dtcs.description: A/F Sensor (Sensor 1) Circuit Range/Performance Problem The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) monitors the internal 5 V power supply by comparing it to the A/D input power supply voltage (3.45 V). When the 5 V power supply is normal, the comparison power supply voltage (3.45 V) falls within a normal area. But if the 5 V power supply malfunctions, the comparison power supply voltage (3.45 V) exceeds the range of normal area. If the comparison power supply voltage is out of permissible range for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ECM/PCM internal comparison power supply voltage is 3.13 V or less, 3.84 V or more for at least 2.0 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Test-drive under these conditions: * Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) * A/T in D, M/T in 3rd * Vehicle speed between 25-55 mph (40-90 km/h) for 5 minutes * Drive at a steady speed between 55-75 mph (88- 120 km/h) for 10 seconds, then decelerate (with throttle fully closed) for 4 seconds or more -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P2A00 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- Replace the A/F sensor (Sensor 1)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Oxygen Sensor/Service and Repair/A/F Sensor Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/F sensor (Sensor 1) and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep driving until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 1-3 and recheck. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Test-drive under these conditions: * Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) * A/T in D, M/T in 3rd * Vehicle speed between 25-55 mph (40-90 km/h) for 5 minutes * Drive at a steady speed between 55-75 mph (88- 120 km/h) for 10 seconds, then decelerate (with throttle fully closed) for 4 seconds or more -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P2A00 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, EXECUTING, or OUT OF CONDITION? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. -EXECUTING- Keep driving until a result comes on. -OUT OF CONDITION- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P06A8: Internal VCC Power Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P06A8

dtcs.description: Internal VCC Power Malfunction The alternator is driven by the engine, and it generates electricity according to the commands from the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The LIN line is connected to the alternator and transmits/receives information to/ from the ECM/PCM. The ECM/PCM sets a target generating voltage in the range of 12.5 V to 14.5 V and commands the alternator via the LIN line. The alternator output signal (duty signal) is sent to the ECM/PCM, and it is varied according to the battery's state of charge, the electrical load, and the engine speed. If the FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT terminal voltage and the alternator output are out of a specified value for a set time while the ECM/PCM commands a target generating voltage to 14.5 V at a specified engine speed, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT terminal voltage is 12.0 V or less, and the alternator power generation amount is within 1.0 % to 50.0 % for at least 60
seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P06A8 indicated? -YES- Replace the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16BB: Alternator B Terminal Circuit Low Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)
Start the engine.
Maintain an engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] of 500 rpm to 3,000 rpm.

Turn on the headlights (high beam) and rear window defogger. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the ECM/PCM memory. The MIL does not come on. Conditions for clearing the DTC The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P16BB

dtcs.description: Alternator B Terminal Circuit Low Voltage The local interconnect network (LIN) line connects the alternator and the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The LIN uses a master/slave protocol to send and receive data to the different components on the line. The ECM/PCM is the master unit, which controls the engine electrical system. The battery sensor unit and the alternator are the slaves. The alternator receives data from the ECM/PCM and provides feedback to the ECM/PCM, which allows the ECM/ PCM to operate the engine electrical system efficiently. When the ECM/PCM does not receive signals from the alternator within its specified amount of time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: Either one of the conditions is met for at least 5 seconds:
The ECM/PCM cannot receive any information via the LIN line.
The information sent from the alternator is abnormal.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. -4. Check under these conditions: * A/C on * Temperature control at maximum cool * Blower fan at maximum speed * Headlights on high beam * Rear window defogger on -5. Hold the engine speed at 2,000 rpm (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) for 1 minute. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16BB indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator and the under-hood fuse/relay box. Also check the battery performance  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/Battery Test.

+B terminal check: +B terminal check -1. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator and the under-hood fuse/relay box (+B line). Are the connections and terminals OK? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Repair the connections or the terminals, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (+B line): Open wire check (+B line) -1. Check for an open in the wire between the alternator and the under-hood fuse/relay box at the engine harness. Is the harness OK? -YES- Replace the alternator  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Alternator/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the +B wire between the alternator and the under-hood fuse/relay box, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Start the engine. -5. Check under these conditions: * A/C on * Temperature control at maximum cool * Blower fan at maximum speed * Headlights on high beam * Rear window defogger on -6. Hold the engine speed at 2,000 rpm (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) for 1 minute. -7. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16BB indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator and the under-hood fuse/relay box, then go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16E2: PGM-FI-ACG LIN Communication Error (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P16E2

dtcs.description: PGM-FI-ACG LIN Communication Error The local interconnect network (LIN) line connects the battery sensor and the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The LIN uses a master/slave protocol to send and receive data to the different components on the line. The ECM/PCM is the master unit, which controls the engine electrical system. The battery sensor unit and the alternator are the slaves. The battery sensor receives data from the ECM/PCM and provides feedback to the ECM/PCM, which allows the ECM/PCM to operate the engine electrical system efficiently. When the ECM/PCM does not receive signals from the battery sensor within its specified amount of time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: Either one of the conditions is met for at least 5 seconds:
The ECM/PCM cannot receive any information via the LIN line.
The information sent from the battery sensor is abnormal.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS, and wait for 10 seconds. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E2 indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator, the battery sensor, and the ECM/PCM.
DTC Check: DTC Check -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E3 indicated? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Go to step 5.

Determine possible failure area (LIN circuit shorted, others): Determine possible failure area (LIN circuit shorted, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 6.

Shorted wire check (LIN line): Shorted wire check (LIN line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Continue to check for continuity between battery sensor 2P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground, while disconnecting these connectors, one at a time: * Alternator 1P connector * ECM/PCM connector B (49P) Does continuity go away when one of the above connectors is disconnected? -YES- The LIN wire is OK. Replace the part that caused an open when it was disconnected, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair short in the LIN wire between the battery sensor, alternator, and the ECM/PCM (B16), then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (LIN line): Open wire check (LIN line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connectors. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The LIN wire is OK. Replace the alternator  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Alternator/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the LIN wire between the alternator and the ECM/PCM (B16), then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (LIN line): Open wire check (LIN line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The LIN wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the LIN wire between the alternator and the ECM/PCM (B16), then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E2 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator, the battery sensor, and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16E3: PGM-FI-Battery Sensor LIN Communication Error (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P16E3

dtcs.description: PGM-FI-Battery Sensor LIN Communication Error The alternator is driven by the engine, and it generates electricity according to the commands from the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The LIN line is connected to the alternator and transmits/receives information to/ from the ECM/PCM. The ECM/PCM sets a target generating voltage in the range of 12.5 V to 14.5 V and commands the alternator via the LIN line. The alternator output signal (duty signal) is sent to the ECM/PCM, and is varied according to the battery's state of charge, the electrical load, and the engine speed. The voltage regulator in the alternator runs a self-diagnostic function by ECM/PCM command, the results are transmitted via the LIN line. If the self-diagnosed results are judged that the alternator temperature is too high, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The alternator internal temperature is 190 °C (374 °F) or more for at least 60 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS, and wait for 10 seconds. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E3 indicated? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator, the battery sensor, and the ECM/PCM.
DTC Check: DTC Check -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E2 indicated? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Go to step 6.

Fuse check: Fuse check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check the following fuse. Is the fuse OK? -YES- The fuse is OK. Go to step 4. -NO- Repair a short in the +B_HORN wire between the battery sensor and the under-hood fuse/relay box and replace the No. A29 (10 A) fuse, then do the repair verification.

Determine possible failure area (LIN circuit shorted, others): Determine possible failure area (LIN circuit shorted, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Go to step 8.

Shorted wire check (LIN line): Shorted wire check (LIN line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Continue to check for continuity between the alternator 1P connector terminal and body ground, while disconnecting these connectors, one at a time: * Battery sensor 2P connector * ECM/PCM connector B (49P) Does continuity go away when one of the above connectors is disconnected? -YES- The LIN wire is OK. Replace the part that caused an open when it was disconnected, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair short in the LIN wire between the battery sensor, alternator, and the ECM/PCM (B16), then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (+B_HORN line): Open wire check (+B_HORN line) -1. Disconnect the following connector. -2. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The +B_HORN wire is OK. Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open in the +B_HORN wire between the battery sensor and No. A29 fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box. Also check the No. A29 fuse, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (LIN line): Open wire check (LIN line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The LIN wire is OK. Replace the battery sensor  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Battery Sensor Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the LIN wire between the battery sensor and the ECM/PCM (B16), then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (LIN line): Open wire check (LIN line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The LIN wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the LIN wire between the battery sensor and the ECM/PCM (B16), then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II) -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E3 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the alternator, the battery sensor, and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16E4: ACG High-temperature (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P16E4

dtcs.description: ACG High-temperature The quick warm-up system supplies additional air and retards the ignition timing when the engine is cold to activate the catalytic converter as quickly as possible. When the actual amount of air is less than the target amount, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The total airflow is decreased by a factor of 0.59 for at least 10 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. If the temperature of the engine and the alternator is high, open the hood to cool the engine compartment. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16E4 indicated? -YES- Replace the alternator  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Alternator/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement. -NO- Check the cause of the high temperature, and repair it. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P050A: Cold Start Idle Air Control System Performance Problem (Engine Type: R18Z1)
Allow the engine to cool to an ambient engine coolant temperature [ECT SENSOR 1] of 140 °F (60 °C) or less.

Start the engine, and let it idle for at least 10 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P050A

dtcs.description: Cold Start Idle Air Control System Performance Problem The quick warm-up system supplies additional air and retards the ignition timing when the engine is cold to activate the catalytic converter as quickly as possible. When the actual engine speed is a specified fast idle value, and it continues for a specified time, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] is 2,400 rpm or more for at least 3.5 seconds.

steps.instruction: PGM-FI system check: PGM-FI system check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Are any Pending or Confirmed DTCs other than P050A indicated? -YES- Go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -NO- Go to step 2.

Intake air duct visual check: Intake air duct visual check -1. Check for a poor connection or blockage at the intake air duct. Is it OK? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Reconnect or repair the intake air duct, then do the repair verification.

Air cleaner housing visual check: Air cleaner housing visual check -1. Check for a poor connection or blockage at the air cleaner housing. Is OK? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Replace the air cleaner housing  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Tune-up and Engine Performance Checks/Air Cleaner Housing/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Air cleaner element visual check: Air cleaner element visual check -1. Check for dirt or debris on the air cleaner element. Is it dirty? -YES- Replace the air cleaner element  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Tune-up and Engine Performance Checks/Air Cleaner Housing/Air Filter Element/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 5.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Let the engine cool until the value of ECT SENSOR 1 is 122 °F (50 °C) or less. -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle for 10 seconds or more. -4. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P050A with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 6. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the throttle body, the MAF sensor/IAT sensor, and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep idling until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 5-1. and recheck.

Throttle body test: Throttle body test -1. Do the ETCS TEST with the HDS. Is the THROTTLE ACTUATOR CONTROL VALVE normal? -YES- Go to step 7. -NO- Replace the throttle body  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement, then do the repair verification.

MAF sensor performance check: MAF sensor performance check -1. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -2. While holding the engine speed at 2,500 rpm for 30 seconds, check the MAF SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 5.3-6.5 g/s -YES- The MAF sensor is OK. Go to step 8. -NO- Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.
IAT sensor performance check: IAT sensor performance check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Allow the engine to cool to ambient temperature. -3.
Allow the engine to cool to an ambient engine coolant temperature [ECT SENSOR 1] of 140 °F (60 °C) or less.

Start the engine, and let it idle for at least 10 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P050B

dtcs.description: Cold Start Ignition Timing Control System Performance Problem When a malfunction occurs in the communication line between the main CPU and the sub CPU in the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM), no monitor signals from the sub CPU for a set time, or abnormal signals are detected for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: One of these symptoms occurs for at least 2.0 seconds:
No sub CPU monitor signals are detected.
Sub CPU monitor signals are abnormal.

steps.instruction: PGM-FI system check: PGM-FI system check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Are any Pending or Confirmed DTCs other than P050B indicated? -YES- Go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -NO- Only DTC P050B is indicated. Go to step 2.

Intake air duct visual check: Intake air duct visual check -1. Check for a poor connection or blockage at the intake air duct. Is OK? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Reconnect or repair the intake air duct, then do the repair verification.

Air cleaner housing visual check: Air cleaner housing visual check -1. Check for a poor connection or blockage at the air cleaner housing. Is OK? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Replace the air cleaner housing  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Tune-up and Engine Performance Checks/Air Cleaner Housing/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Air cleaner element visual check: Air cleaner element visual check -1. Check for dirt or debris on the air cleaner element. Is dirty? -YES- Replace the air cleaner element  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Tune-up and Engine Performance Checks/Air Cleaner Housing/Air Filter Element/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 5.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Let the engine cool until the value of ECT SENSOR 1 is 122 °F (50 °C) or less. -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle for 10 seconds or more. -4. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P050B with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 6. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the CKP sensor, the throttle body, the MAF sensor/IAT sensor, ECT sensor 1, ECT sensor 2, and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep idling until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 5-1. and recheck.

Determine possible failure area (CKP sensor, others): Determine possible failure area (CKP sensor, others) -1. Check the ignition timing  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Tune-up and Engine Performance Checks/Ignition Timing/Testing and Inspection. Is the ignition timing OK? -YES- Go to step 8. -NO- Go to step 7.

CKP sensor and CKP pulse plate visual check: CKP sensor and CKP sensor plate visual check: -1. Check for damage at the CKP sensor and the CKP pulse plate. Is the CKP sensor or the CKP pulse plate damaged? -YES- Replace the CKP sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Crankshaft Position Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/CKP Sensor Removal and Installation and/or the CKP pulse plate  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Cylinder Block Assembly/Crankshaft/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Throttle body test: Throttle body test -1. Do the ETCS TEST with the HDS. Is the THROTTLE ACTUATOR CONTROL VALVE normal? -YES- The throttle body is OK. Go to step 9. -NO- Replace the throttle body  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement, then do the repair verification.

MAF sensor performance check: MAF sensor performance check -1. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -2. While holding the engine speed at 2,500 rpm for 30 seconds, check the MAF SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 5.3-6.5 g/s? -YES- The MAF sensor is OK. Go to step 10. -NO- Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

ECT sensor 1 and ECT sensor 2 performance check: ECT sensor 1 and ECT sensor 2 performance check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Allow the engine to cool to the ambient temperature. -3. Control System DTCs P060A

dtcs.description: ECM/PCM Internal Control Module Malfunction The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) is equipped with a keep alive memory (KAM). The data (control learn data, etc.) for powertrain control and information (vehicle identification number (VIN), etc.) related to the vehicle control is stored in the KAM, so that it can be maintained even when power is not supplied to the ECM/PCM, such as when the battery is disconnected. When power is restored to the ECM/PCM, the CPU retrieves the stored information from the KAM, but when the data retrieval process is not finished normally, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The CPU writes data to the KAM from the CPU: Control related data is written when the ignition switch is turned to ON (II), and vehicle information when commanded from the HDS. If the data writing process is not completed normally, the ECM/ PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: A malfunction is detected whenever the keep alive data retrieval and writing process is not completed normally.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P060A indicated? -YES- Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Repair verification: -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P060A indicated? -YES- If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P062F: Engine Control Module (ECM)/Powertrain Control Module (PCM) Internal Control Module Keep Alive Memory (KAM) Error (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P062F

dtcs.description: ECM/PCM Internal Control Module Keep Alive Memory (KAM) Error The alternator is driven by the engine, and it generates electricity according to the commands from the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The LIN line is connected to the alternator and transmits/receives information to/ from the ECM/PCM. The voltage regulator in the alternator self-diagnoses the condition of itself, and by the ECM/PCM demands, the results are transmitted via the LIN line. If the self-diagnosed results are judged as generation unable or alternator rotation stop, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: Either one of the conditions is met for at least 5 seconds:
B terminal voltage is regulated voltage or more, and F terminal voltage is HIGH.
B terminal voltage is 10 V or less (when the alternator speed is 3,000 rpm or less), or 11 V or less (when the alternator speed is 3,000 rpm or
more).
P terminal voltage is 2 V or less (when the alternator speed is 3,000 rpm or less), or 6 V or less (when the alternator speed is 3,000 rpm or more).
Alternator speed is 400 rpm or less.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P062F indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Repair verification: -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P062F indicated? -YES- If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P065A: ACG No Charging Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P065A

dtcs.description: ACG No Charging Malfunction The intake manifold tuning (IMT) actuator is controlled to open/close the IMT valves by the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) to output high torque at any engine speed range. When it is in low engine speed, the IMT valve is closed, and when it is in high engine speed, the IMT valve is opened. When the ECM/PCM detects an abnormal return signal or abnormal current from the IMT actuator, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: Either one of the conditions is met:
Open circuit failure
The return signal from the IMT actuator is HIGH for at least 2 seconds while the current is not applied to the IMT actuator.
Short circuit failure
Over current from the IMT actuator continues for at least 0.4 second while the current is applied to the IMT actuator.

steps.instruction: DTC check: DTC check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0562 indicated at the same time? -YES- Go to DTC P0562 troubleshooting  See: P0562/DTC Troubleshooting. -NO- Go to step 2.

Drive belt check: Drive belt check -1. Inspect the drive belt  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Drive Belts, Mounts, Brackets and Accessories/Drive Belt/Testing and Inspection . Is the drive belt OK? -YES- Repair or replace the alternator  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Alternator/Service and Repair/Overhaul. -NO- Replace the drive belt  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Drive Belts, Mounts, Brackets and Accessories/Drive Belt/Service and Repair. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P065E: Intake Manifold Tuning (IMT) Actuator Circuit Malfunction Control System DTCs P065E

dtcs.description: IMT Actuator Circuit Malfunction The mass airflow (MAF) sensor directly measures the amount of intake airflow into the engine, and the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) controls the fuel injection based on the measured value. The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) sensor measures the intake manifold pressure, and the ECM/PCM calculates the amount of intake airflow from the MAP sensor output and the engine revolutions. The ECM/PCM compares the MAF sensor output (amount of intake airflow) and the amount of intake airflow calculated from MAP sensor output. If their difference is too large (in the malfunction area), the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The difference between the amount of intake air measured by the MAF sensor and the amount of intake air calculated from the MAP sensor output is out
of the normal area for at least 5.0 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Turn the ignition switch OFF (0). -4. Turn the ignition switch ON (II). -5. Select the IMT (IMRC) TEST in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS, and do the IMT (IMRC) ACTIVATION for 10 seconds. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P065E indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the IMT actuator and the ECM/PCM.

Shorted wire check (SVSP line to power): Shorted wire check (SVSP line to power) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 0.1 V or less? -YES- The SVSP wire is not shorted to power. Go to step 3. -NO- Repair a short to power in the SVSP wire between the ECM/PCM (B12) and the IMT actuator, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (SVSM line to power): Shorted wire check (SVSM line to power) -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 0.1 V or less? -YES- The SVSM wire is not shorted to power. Go to step 4. -NO- Repair a short to power in the SVSM wire between the ECM/PCM (B13) and the IMT actuator, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (SVSP line to ground): Shorted wire check (SVSP line to ground) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 1.0 MOhms or more? -YES- The SVSP wire is not shorted to ground. Go to step 5. -NO- Repair a short to ground in the SVSP wire between the ECM/PCM (B12) and the IMT actuator, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (SVSM line to ground): Shorted wire check (SVSM line to ground) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 1.0 MOhms or more? -YES- The SVSM wire is not shorted to ground. Go to step 6. -NO- Repair a short to ground in the SVSM wire between the ECM/PCM (B13) and the IMT actuator, then do the repair verification.

Determination of possible failure area (ECM/PCM, IMT circuit shorted, IMT circuit open): Determination of possible failure area (ECM/PCM, IMT circuit shorted, IMT circuit open) -1. Measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 15-30 Ohms? -15 Ohms or less- The IMT circuit is shorted. Go to step 7. -15-30 Ohms- The SVSP/SVSM wire are OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -30 Ohms or more- The IMT circuit is open. Go to step 8.

IMT circuit internal resistance check: IMT circuit internal resistance check -1. Disconnect the following connector. -2. At the IMT actuator side, measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 15-30 Ohms? -YES- The IMA actuator is OK. Repair a short in the SVSP wire to the SVSM wire between the ECM/PCM (B12, B13) and the IMT actuator, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the IMT actuator  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Variable Induction System/Variable Induction Control Actuator/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

IMT circuit internal resistance check: IMT circuit internal resistance check -1. Disconnect the following connector. -2. At the IMT actuator side, measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 15-30 Ohms? -YES- Repair an open in the SVSP/SVSM wires between the ECM/PCM (B12, B13) and the IMT actuator, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the IMT actuator,  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Variable Induction System/Variable Induction Control Actuator/Service and Repair then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Reconnect all connectors. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -4. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -5. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -6. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -7. Select the IMT (IMRC) TEST in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS, and do the IMT (IMRC) ACTIVATION for 10 seconds. -8. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P065E indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the IMT actuator and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0101: Mass Airflow (MAF) Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Problem (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.
Let the engine idle for at least 5 seconds.

Drive the vehicle at a steady speed of 40 mph (64 km/h) or more for at least 5 seconds. - When freeze data is stored, drive the vehicle under those conditions instead of Driving Pattern step 3. - When you have difficulty duplicating the DTC because of road conditions and traffic situations, repeat the driving pattern several times. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0101

dtcs.description: MAF Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Problem The mass airflow (MAF) sensor is attached to the intake air passage, and it measures the amount of intake airflow. The MAF sensor is a hot wire airflow meter. The airflow cools the electrically heated wire that is mounted in the air passage. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) determines the amount of intake airflow by detecting the current that is required to keep the hot wire at a constant temperature. The lower limit of the MAF sensor output is specified. If the output is below that limit, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The MAF sensor output voltage [MAF SENSOR] is 0.10 V or less for at least 2.0 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Parts condition check: Parts condition check -1. Check for poor connections or damage to these parts: * PCV hose * Intake air duct * Air cleaner * Purge (PCS) line Are the parts OK? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Repair or replace the damaged part(s), then do the repair verification.

Intake air duct visual check: Intake air duct visual check -1. Check for damage or looseness of the air duct in the air cleaner. Is it OK? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Reconnect or replace the air duct in the air cleaner, then do the repair verification.

Air cleaner element visual check: Air cleaner element visual check -1. Check for a dirty air cleaner element. Is it dirty? -YES- Replace the air cleaner element  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Tune-up and Engine Performance Checks/Air Cleaner Housing/Air Filter Element/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 4.

MAF sensor signal check (without engine running): MAF sensor signal check (without engine running) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Check the MAF SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 0.2 g/s or 0.5 V? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

MAF sensor signal check (with engine running): MAF sensor signal check (with engine running) -1. Start the engine. -2. Vary the engine speed between 2,000 rpm and 3,000 rpm. -3. Check the MAF SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the reading change? -YES- Go to step 6. -NO- Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

MAF sensor check: MAF sensor check -1. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -2. Test-drive the vehicle for several minutes in the range of these recorded freeze data parameters: * ENGINE SPEED * VEHICLE SPEED * MAP SENSOR * TP SENSOR -3. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0101 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates NOT COMPLETED, go to step 6-2 and recheck. Repair verification (OBD STATUS): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure -4. Test-drive the vehicle for several minutes in the range of these recorded freeze data parameters: * ENGINE SPEED * VEHICLE SPEED * MAP SENSOR * TP SENSOR -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0101 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0102: Mass Airflow (MAF) Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0102

dtcs.description: MAF Sensor Circuit Low Voltage The mass airflow (MAF) sensor is attached to the intake air passage, and it measures the amount of intake airflow. The MAF sensor is a hot wire airflow meter. The airflow cools the electrically heated wire that is mounted in the air passage. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) determines the amount of intake airflow by detecting the current that is required to keep the hot wire at a constant temperature. The upper limit of the MAF sensor output is specified. If the output is above that limit, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The MAF sensor output voltage [MAF SENSOR] is 4.89 V or more for at least 2.0 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and wait 2 seconds. -2. Check the MAF SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is about 0 g/s, or 0.1 V or less indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the ECM/PCM.

Shorted wire check (IG1_ACG, IG1(AFM) lines) Shorted wire check (IG1_ACG, IG1(AFM) lines) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check the following fuse. Is the fuse OK? -YES- The IG1_ACG/IG1(AFM) wires are not shorted. Go to step 3. -NO- Repair a short in the IG1_ACG/IG1(AFM) wire between the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the No. B2 (15 A) fuse. Also replace the No. B2 (15 A) fuse, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (IG1(AFM) line): Open wire check (IG1(AFM) line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The IG1(AFM) wire is OK. Go to step 4. -NO- Repair an open in the IG1(AFM) wire between the No. B2 (15 A) fuse and the MAF sensor/IAT sensor, then do the repair verification.

Determine possible failure area (VGP line, others): Determine possible failure area (VGP line, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there 190-210 kOhms? -YES- Go to step 7. -NO- Go to step 5.

Shorted wire check (VGP line): Shorted wire check (VGP line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the VGP wire between the ECM/PCM (B22) and the MAF sensor/IAT sensor, then do the repair verification. -NO- The VGP wire is not shorted. Go to step 6.

Open wire check (VGP line): Open wire check (VGP line) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The VGP wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the VGP wire between the ECM/PCM (B22) and the MAF sensor/IAT sensor, then do the repair verification.

MAF sensor/IAT sensor check (substitution): MAF sensor/IAT sensor check (substitution) -1. Substitute a known-good MAF sensor/IAT sensor.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -5. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 2,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral). -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0102 indicated? -YES- The MAF sensor/IAT sensor is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the original MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0102 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0103: Mass Airflow (MAF) Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0103

dtcs.description: MAF Sensor Circuit High Voltage The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) sensor measures the pressure or vacuum in the manifold. This measurement is converted into an electrical signal which the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) uses to run the engine. The MAP sensor outputs low signal voltage at high vacuum (throttle valve closed) and high signal voltage at low vacuum (throttle valve wide open). If the signal voltage from the MAP sensor is a set value, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The MAP sensor output voltage [MAP SENSOR] is 0.23 V or less for at least 2 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and wait 2 seconds. -2. Check the MAF SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is about 202 g/s, or 4.89 V or more indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the ECM/PCM.

Open wire check (VGM line): Open wire check (VGM line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connectors. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The VGM wire is OK. Go to step 3. -NO- Repair an open in the VGM wire between the ECM/PCM (B43) and the MAF sensor/IAT sensor, then do the repair verification.

ECM/PCM internal circuit check: ECM/PCM internal circuit check -1. Reconnect the following connector. -2. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -5. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0103 indicated? -YES- Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- The ECM/PCM internal circuit is OK. Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0107: Manifold Absolute Pressure (MAP) Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0107

dtcs.description: MAP Sensor Circuit Low Voltage The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) sensor measures the pressure or vacuum in the manifold. This measurement is converted into an electrical signal which the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) uses to run the engine. The MAP sensor outputs low signal voltage at high vacuum (throttle valve closed) and high signal voltage at low vacuum (throttle valve wide open). If the signal voltage from the MAP sensor is a set value, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The MAP sensor output voltage [MAP SENSOR] is 4.49 V or more for at least 2 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

dtcs.description: MAP Sensor Circuit High Voltage Two engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensors and one intake air temperature (IAT) sensor are used by the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). When the engine is stopped and enough time has passed, the temperature of the engine will equalize to the ambient temperature. ECT sensor 1 and IAT sensor are compared to ECT sensor 2, and if an inappropriate temperature is read, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction in the corresponding sensor and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: A malfunction is detected if the following conditions are present after the engine has stopped and the ignition switch has been turned to LOCK (0), for at
least 6 hours before restarting the engine:

Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0111: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Problem (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and wait at least 6 hours.

Start the engine, and let it idle for at least 10 seconds. - Do not use an engine block heater because the engine coolant temperature will not stay equal during engine stop. - When freeze data is stored, test the vehicle under an ambient temperature close to the intake air temperature in the freeze data. - When an ambient temperature extremely changes, malfunction judgment may not execute. Avoid extreme temperature changing condition after or before engine start. (When the vehicle is parked in the garage, open the shutter to introduce outside air; then start the engine.) Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0111

dtcs.description: IAT Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Problem The intake air temperature (IAT) sensor is a thermistor that detects intake air temperature, and it is used for air/fuel ratio feedback control to compensate for the atmospheric density fluctuations that accompany changes in intake air temperature. The IAT sensor resistance varies depending on temperature. The output voltage and the sensor resistance increase as the intake air temperature decreases. Conversely, the output voltage and the sensor resistance decrease as the intake air temperature increases. If the IAT sensor output voltage is excessively low, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The IAT sensor output voltage [IAT SENSOR (2)] is 0.08 V or less for at least 2.0 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Connector visual check: Connector visual check -1. Check for poor or loose connections and terminals at these locations: * MAF sensor/IAT sensor * ECT sensor 1 * ECT sensor 2 Are the connections and terminals OK? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Repair the connections or terminals, then do the repair verification.

IAT sensor performance check (low temperature): IAT sensor performance check (low temperature) -1. Remove the MAF sensor/IAT sensor.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair -2. Allow the MAF sensor/IAT sensor to cool to the ambient temperature. -3.

IAT sensor performance check (high temperature): IAT sensor performance check (high temperature) -1. Disconnect the following connector. -2. Using a heat gun, blow hot air on the MAF sensor/IAT sensor for a few seconds. Do not apply the heat longer than a few seconds or you will damage the sensor. -3. Connect the MAF sensor/IAT sensor to its 5P connector, but do not install it. -4. Check the IAT SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the IAT SENSOR change 76 °F (42 °C) or more? -YES- Replace the MAF sensor/IAT sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Air Flow Meter/Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the ECM/PCM. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0111 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MAF sensor/IAT sensor and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0113: Intake Air Temperature (IAT) Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0113

dtcs.description: IAT Sensor Circuit High Voltage The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) supplies voltage to the engine coolant temperature signal circuit (about 5 V) through a pull-up resistor. As the engine coolant cools, the engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensor 1 resistance increases, and the ECM/PCM detects a high signal voltage. As the engine coolant warms, the ECT sensor 1 resistance decreases, and the ECM/PCM detects a low signal voltage. If the ECT sensor 1 output voltage does not reach a set temperature after starting the engine and driving a set time, or when the difference between the ECT sensor 1 output voltage while driving and the output voltage of ECT sensor 1 after the engine has been stopped a certain length of time does not change a certain amount, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: (Engine Type: R18Z1)
Malfunction determination 1:
With a completely cooled engine (one that has been off for at least 6 hours):
When the change in coolant temperature after 24 minutes or more of running time or 3 miles (4 km) or more of driving distance is 18 °F (10 °C) or less,
a malfunction is detected.
Malfunction determination 2:
With a partially cooled engine (one that has been off for less than 6 hours):
When the difference between the coolant temperature after 24 minutes or more of running time or 3 miles (4 km) or more of driving distance the coolant
temperature after the engine has been off for 4 hours and then run for 10 seconds is 18 °F (10 °C) or less, a malfunction is detected.
(Engine Type: R18A9)
Malfunction determination 1:
With a completely cooled engine (one that has been off for at least 6 hours):
When the change in coolant temperature after 48 minutes or more of running time or 5 miles (8 km) or more of driving distance is 18 °F (10 °C) or less,
a malfunction is detected.
Malfunction determination 2:
With a partially cooled engine (one that has been off for less than 6 hours):
When the difference between the coolant temperature after 48 minutes or more of running time or 5 miles (8 km) or more of driving distance the coolant
temperature after the engine has been off for 6 hours and then run for 10 seconds is 18 °F (10 °C) or less, a malfunction is detected.

dtcs.description: ECT Sensor 1 Circuit Range/Performance Problem Engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensor 1 is used for air/fuel ratio feedback control, ignition timing control, idle speed control, and other functions. The ECT sensor 1 resistance varies depending on the engine coolant temperature. As the engine coolant cools, the ECT sensor 1 resistance increases, and the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a high signal voltage. As the engine coolant warms, the ECT sensor 1 resistance decreases, and the ECM/PCM detects a low signal voltage. If the ECT sensor 1 output voltage is a set value, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ECT sensor 1 output voltage [ECT SENSOR 1] is 0.08 V or less for at least 2 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

dtcs.description: TP Sensor A Circuit High Voltage The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) supplies voltage to the engine coolant temperature signal circuit (about 5 V) through a pull-up resistor. As the engine coolant cools, the engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensor 1 resistance increases, and the ECM/PCM detects a high signal voltage. As the engine coolant warms, the ECT sensor 1 resistance decreases, and the ECM/PCM detects a low signal voltage. If the ECT sensor 1 output voltage does not reach a specified temperature at which closed loop control for stoichiometric air/fuel ratio starts within a set time, depending on the initial engine coolant temperature after starting the engine, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. If the ECT sensor 1 output voltage does not reach a specified temperature at which closed loop control for stoichiometric air/fuel ratio starts within a set time, depending on the initial engine coolant temperature after starting the engine, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.

KA, KL, KC Models Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0125: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Sensor 1 Malfunction/Slow Response (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)
Start the engine under Enable Conditions (see "Initial engine coolant temperature [ECT SENSOR 1]").

Let the engine idle for at least 20 minutes. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. KX Models Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0125: Engine Coolant Temperature (ECT) Sensor 1 Malfunction/Slow Response (Engine Type: R18Z1)

If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. KA, KL, KC Models Control System DTCs P0125

dtcs.description: ECT Sensor 1 Malfunction/Slow Response The thermostat is closed when the engine coolant temperature is low, and it stops the circulation of engine coolant to speed engine warm up. When the engine coolant temperature increases, the thermostat opens and circulates engine coolant to control its temperature. When the engine coolant temperature decreases, the opening area of the thermostat is reduced to regulate the engine coolant temperature. If the thermostat sticks open, engine warm up is delayed, and exhaust emissions are adversely affected. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) measures the rise in the coolant temperature after the engine starts at the engine block and at the radiator, and it estimates the characteristics of the engine coolant temperature by calculations based on those two temperatures and the driving conditions. When ECT 2 immediately increases from the starting value, it is defined as the thermostat stuck open. When ECT 2 does not increase to the specified value, it is defined as a thermostat malfunction.
detection_condition: Malfunction determination 1:
If the difference between the current measured engine coolant temperature at the radiator (ECT 2) [ECT SENSOR 2] and the initial engine coolant
temperature at the radiator (ECT 2) [ECT SENSOR 2] is at least 9 °F (5 °C)*1, (13 °F (7 °C))*2 when the estimated engine coolant temperature at the
engine (ECT 1) [ECT SENSOR 1] reaches 72 °F (22 °C), a malfunction is detected (thermostat stuck open); or if the engine coolant temperature at the
radiator (ECT 2) [ECT SENSOR 2] only reaches 163 °F (73 °C), a malfunction is detected (thermostat malfunction).
Malfunction determination 2:
When the calculated engine coolant temperature at the engine (ECT 1) [ECT SENSOR 1] reaches 158 °F (70 °C) before the measured engine coolant
temperature at the engine (ECT 1) [ECT SENSOR 1] reaches 158 °F (70 °C), a malfunction is detected.

steps.instruction: ECT sensor 1 check: ECT sensor 1 check -1. Start the engine, and let it idle for 5 minutes or more. -2. Check ECT SENSOR 1 in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is about 0 °F (-12 °C) or less indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at ECT sensor 1, ECT sensor 2, and the ECM/PCM. If they are OK, replace ECT sensor 1  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Engine - Coolant Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair/ECT Sensor 1 Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 2.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Allow the engine to cool to 104 °F (40 °C) or less. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle until ECT SENSOR 1 goes up to about 158 °F (70 °C). -4. Check ECT SENSOR 2 in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Does ECT SENSOR 2 also read about 158 °F (70 °C)? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 3. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at ECT sensor 1, ECT sensor 2, and the ECM/PCM.

Thermostat check: Thermostat check -1. Check the thermostat  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Thermostat/Service and Repair/Thermostat Removal, Installation, and Test. Is the thermostat OK? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at ECT sensor 1, ECT sensor 2, and the ECM/PCM. If the connections and terminals are OK, replace ECT sensor 1  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Engine - Coolant Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair/ECT Sensor 1 Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the thermostat  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Cooling System/Thermostat/Service and Repair/Thermostat Removal, Installation, and Test, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -4. Allow the engine to cool to 104 °F (40 °C) or less. -5. Start the engine, and let it idle until ECT SENSOR 1 goes up to about 158 °F (70 °C). -6. Check ECT SENSOR 2 in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Does ECT SENSOR 2 also read about 158 °F (70 °C)? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at ECT sensor 1, ECT sensor 2, and the ECM/PCM, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. KX Models Control System DTCs P0125

dtcs.description: Cooling System Malfunction The air/fuel ratio (A/F) sensor (sensor 1) has a linear signal output in relation to the oxygen concentration. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) computes the air/fuel ratio from the A/F sensor output voltage and uses fuel feedback control to improve exhaust emissions. The ECM/PCM compares the response characteristics against the A/F sensor output. If the total A/F sensor output is a specified value for a specified time, the ECM/PCM detects a deteriorated response and stores a DTC. The air/fuel ratio (A/F) sensor (sensor 1) has a linear signal output in relation to the oxygen concentration. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) computes the air/fuel ratio from the A/F sensor output voltage and uses fuel feedback control to improve exhaust emissions. The ECM/PCM compares the response characteristics against the A/F sensor output. If the total A/F sensor output is a specified value for a specified time, the ECM/PCM detects a deteriorated response and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: (Engine Type: R18Z1)
The total A/F sensor (sensor 1) output value is 0.00104* or less in 7.0 seconds.
*: Depending on engine speed and load.
(Engine Type: R18A9)
The total A/F sensor (sensor 1) output value is 57 or less in 4.5 seconds.
The total A/F sensor (sensor 1) output value is 30 or less in 7.0 seconds.

steps.instruction: Radiator fan control command check: Radiator fan control command check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Turn the blower switch off. -4.
