dtcs.description: No. 1 ignition coil IGNO1A line open - No. 1 ignition coil IGNO1A line short to ground
No. 2 ignition coil IGNO2A line open - No. 2 ignition coil IGNO2A line short to ground
No. 3 ignition coil IGNO3A line open - No. 3 ignition coil IGNO3A line short to ground

No. 4 ignition coil IGNO4A line open - No. 4 ignition coil IGNO4A line short to ground Common - Ignition coil power supply line open - Ignition coil ground line open - ECM/PCM internal circuit failure - Ignition coil internal circuit failure Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the ECM/PCM memory. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0351, P0352, P0353, P0354
detection_condition: The return signal does not change for at least 5 seconds when the ignition coil is triggered.
No input signals from the CMP sensor are detected while signals from the CKP sensor are detected at least 300 times.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0351, P0352, P0353, and/or P0354 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ignition coil and the ECM/PCM.

Ignition coil check: Ignition coil check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Exchange the ignition coil from the problem cylinder with one from another cylinder. -3. Start the engine. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0351, P0352, P0353, or P0354 indicated at the exchanged position? -YES- Replace the faulty ignition coil  See: Powertrain Management/Ignition System/Ignition Coil/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 3.

Shorted wire check (IG1(COIL) line): Shorted wire check (IG1(COIL) line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check the following fuse. Is the fuses OK? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Repair a short in the IG1(COIL) wire between the ignition coil and the ignition coil relay. Replace the No. A16 (15 A) fuse, then do the repair verification.

Relay check: Relay check -1. Remove the ignition coil relay (A) from the under-hood fuse/relay box. -2. Test the ignition coil relay  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is the relay OK? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the ignition coil relay, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (IG1(COIL) lines): Open wire check (IG1(COIL) lines) -1. Reinstall the ignition coil relay. -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The IG1(COIL) wires are OK. Go to step 6. -NO- Repair an open in the IG1(COIL) wire between the ignition coil and the No.16 IG COIL (15 A) fuse, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (PG line): Open wire check (PG line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The PG wire is OK. Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open in the PG wire between the ignition coil and G101, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (IGNO lines): Shorted wire check (IGNO lines) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connectors. -3. Check for continuity between body ground and the appropriate ECM/PCM connector terminal of the problem ignition coil (see table). Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the IGNO wire between the ECM/PCM and the ignition coil, then do the repair verification. -NO- The IGNO wires are not shorted. Go to step 8.

Open wire check (IGNO lines): Open wire check (IGNO lines) -1. Check for continuity between appropriate ignition coil 3P connector terminal No.1 and the appropriate ECM/PCM connector terminal of the problem ignition coil (see table). Is there continuity? -YES- The IGNO wires are OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the IGNO wire between the ECM/PCM and the ignition coil, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0351, P0352, P0353, or P0354 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ignition coil and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0351: No. 1 Cylinder Ignition Coil Circuit Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0352: No. 2 Cylinder Ignition Coil Circuit Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0353: No. 3 Cylinder Ignition Coil Circuit Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0354: No. 4 Cylinder Ignition Coil Circuit Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)

dtcs.description: CMP Sensor Circuit No Signal The camshaft position (CMP) sensor consists of a rotor and a semiconductor that detects rotor position. When the rotor turns after starting the engine, the changes of magnetic flux in the semiconductor are converted into pulsing signals to the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The CMP sensor detects the top dead center of each cylinder for fuel injection timing. If CMP sensor pulsing signals are detected an abnormal number of times due to noise, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: Other than 6 CMP pulses are detected during two crankshaft revolutions. This condition has been detected at least 30 times.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0365 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the CMP sensor and the ECM/PCM.

Open wire check (IGP(TDC) line): Open wire check (IGP(TDC) line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The IGP(TDC) wire is OK. Go to step 3. -NO- Repair an open in the IGP(TDC) wire between the CMP sensor and PGM-FI main relay 1, then do the repair verification.

Determine possible failure area (TDC line, others): Determine possible failure area (TDC line, others) -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 5.0 V? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 5.

Open wire check (LG line): Open wire check (LG line) -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The LG wire is OK. Replace the CMP sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Camshaft Position Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the LG wire between the CMP sensor and G101, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (TDC line): Shorted wire check (TDC line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the TDC wire between the ECM/PCM (C41) and the CMP sensor, then do the repair verification. -NO- The TDC wire is not shorted. Go to step 6.

Open wire check (TDC line): Open wire check (TDC line) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The TDC wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the TDC wire between the ECM/PCM (C41) and the CMP sensor, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0365 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the CMP sensor and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0369: Camshaft Position (CMP) Sensor Circuit Intermittent Interruption (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0369

dtcs.description: CMP Sensor Circuit Intermittent Interruption The exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) deterioration failure conditions include a sticking lift sensor, restricted passages, and leakage from the external piping. At any specific EGR lift value there is a corresponding air/fuel ratio compensation value. If the air/fuel ratio compensation value is greater than the corresponding EGR lift value, the engine control module (ECM)/ powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The air/fuel ratio (A/F) sensor response parameter according to the opening and closing of the EGR is 36,045 (LT80A) or less.

steps.instruction: PGM-FI system check: PGM-FI system check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle for 10 seconds. -4. Check the CMP B NOISE in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Are 0 counts indicated? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Go to step 2.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Test-drive the vehicle for several minutes in the range of these recorded freeze data parameters: * ENGINE SPEED * VEHICLE SPEED -2. Check the CMP B NOISE in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Are 0 counts indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 3. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the CMP sensor and the ECM/PCM.

Connectors and terminals condition check: Connectors and terminals condition check -1. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at these locations: * CMP sensor * ECM/PCM * Engine ground * Body ground Are the connections and terminals OK? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Repair the connections or terminals, then do the repair verification.

CMP pulse plate visual check: CMP pulse plate visual check -1. Remove the cylinder head cover, and check for damage to the CMP pulse plate. Is the pulse plate damaged? -YES- Replace the CMP pulse plate  See: Engine, Cooling and Exhaust/Engine/Cylinder Block Assembly/Crankshaft/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the CMP sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Camshaft Position Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Start the engine, and let it idle for 10 seconds. -7. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0369 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the CMP sensor and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0400: Exhaust Gas Recirculation (EGR) System Leak Detected
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.
Drive the vehicle at a steady speed of 25 mph (40 km/h) or less, for at least 5 minutes.

Drive immediately at a steady speed of 26 mph (41 km/h) or more and engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] between 1,600 - 4,000 rpm for at least 11 seconds. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0400

dtcs.description: EGR System Leak Detected The exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) valve, which is controlled by the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM), is opened and the exhaust gas flows through the EGR valve, the intake manifold, and the EGR passage. The exhaust gas is circulated into the air/fuel mixture to lower combustion temperatures, thus reducing oxides of nitrogen (NOx) emissions. The EGR flow is inspected as follows. The EGR valve is closed during deceleration with the throttle valve fully closed. Then the ECM/PCM fully opens the EGR valve. After a set time, the ECM/PCM computes the ratio of the present EGR flow to the normal EGR flow by monitoring the fluctuation of the intake manifold pressure between when the EGR valve is fully opened and when it is fully closed. If the EGR flow rate is lower than normal, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ratio of the current EGR flow to the normal EGR flow is 31 % or less for at least 3.5 seconds.
The ratio is derived from the actual and estimated MAP values before and after the EGR valve was opened.
The estimated MAP difference value is shown in the table.

steps.instruction: EGR pipe visual check: EGR pipe visual check -1. Check for a loose or damaged EGR pipe. Is the EGR pipe OK? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Reconnect or replace the EGR pipe  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Tube/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -2. Test-drive under these conditions: * Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) * A/T in D, M/T in 4th * Drive the vehicle at 25 mph (40 km/h) or less for 5 minutes * Drive at a steady speed of 26 mph (41 km/h) or more (engine speed between 1,600-4,000 rpm) for 11 seconds or more. -3. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0400 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Replace the EGR valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for an exhaust gas leak between the EGR pipe and the EGR valve. If the HDS indicates NOT COMPLETED, go to step 2-3 and recheck. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Test-drive under these conditions: * Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) * A/T in D, M/T in 4th * Drive the vehicle at 25 mph (40 km/h) or less for 5 minutes * Drive at a steady speed of 26 mph (41 km/h) or more (engine speed between 1,600-4,000 rpm) for 11 seconds or more. -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0400 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0401: Exhaust Gas Recirculation (EGR) Insufficient Flow
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.
Drive the vehicle at a speed between 15 - 75 mph (24 - 120 km/h) for at least 5 seconds.
Drive at a steady speed between 55 - 75 mph (88 - 120 km/h) for at least 10 seconds.

Decelerate with the throttle valve fully closed for at least 4 seconds. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0401

dtcs.description: EGR Insufficient Flow The exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) valve, which is controlled by the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM), is opened and the exhaust gas flows through the EGR valve, the intake manifold, and the EGR passage. The exhaust gas is circulated into the air/fuel mixture and the mixture is drawn into the combustion chamber to lower combustion temperatures, thus reducing oxides of nitrogen (NOx) emissions. A sensor (lift sensor) is built into the EGR valve and detects the amount of valve lift. The command value for the target valve lift is stored in the ECM/PCM so that exhaust gas recirculation can be optimized according to driving conditions. Comparing this command value with the lift sensor output signal value, the ECM/PCM controls the EGR valve to make the amount of actual valve lift equal to the command value. If the difference between the value of commanded EGR valve lift and actual EGR valve lift is a specified value or more, an abnormality in the EGR valve or the lift sensor output is determined, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The difference between the command value of the amount of EGR valve lift in the ECM/PCM and the actual amount of valve lift is 0.041 in (1.02 mm)
or more for at least 5.0 seconds.

steps.instruction: EGR valve check: EGR valve check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Do the EGR TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Replace the EGR valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Test-drive under these conditions: * Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) * A/T in D, M/T in 4th * Drive the vehicle at a steady speed between 15-75 mph (24-120 km/h) for at least 5 seconds * Drive the vehicle at a steady speed between 55-75 mph (90-120 km/h) for at least 10 seconds * During the drive, decelerate (with the throttle fully closed) for 5 seconds -2. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0401 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- Clean the intake manifold EGR port with throttle plate cleaner. Also, clean the passage inside the EGR valve with throttle plate cleaner, then do the repair verification. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EGR valve and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep driving until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 2-1 on this step and recheck. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Test-drive under these conditions: * Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) * A/T in D, M/T in 4th * Drive the vehicle at a steady speed between 15-75 mph (24-120 km/h) for at least 5 seconds * Drive the vehicle at a steady speed between 55-75 mph (90-120 km/h) for at least 10 seconds * During the drive, decelerate (with the throttle fully closed) for 5 seconds -7. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0401 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, EXECUTING, or OUT OF CONDITION? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. -EXECUTING- Keep the test condition until a result comes on. -OUT OF CONDITION- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0404: Exhaust Gas Recirculation (EGR) Valve Circuit Range/Performance Problem
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.

Drive the vehicle at a speed between 15 - 75 mph (24 - 120 km/h) for at least 5 seconds. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0404

dtcs.description: EGR Valve Circuit Range/Performance Problem The exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) system reduces oxides of nitrogen (NOx). NOx is generated by high combustion temperatures. The EGR system lowers peak combustion temperature by recirculating inert exhaust gas into the air/fuel mixture, thus reducing NOx emissions. To determine the optimal amount of recirculating exhaust gas, depending on driving conditions, a command value (the amount of valve lift) is stored in the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The EGR valve position sensor indicates the amount of valve lift, and the ECM/PCM controls the EGR valve so that the amount of actual valve lift equals the command value by comparing the command value and the actual amount of valve lift. If the EGR valve position sensor output voltage is not within a specified value, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The EGR valve position sensor output voltage is 4.89 V or more for at least 2 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Do the EGR TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Clean any carbon build-up on the EGR valve with throttle plate cleaner. -NO- Failure is duplicated. Go to step 2.

EGR valve position sensor internal circuit check: EGR valve position sensor internal circuit check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. At the EGR valve side, measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there 100 kOhms or more? -YES- Replace the EGR valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 3.

EGR valve internal circuit check: EGR valve internal circuit check -1. At the EGR valve side, measure the resistance between test point 1 and 2. Is there 100 kOhms or more? -YES- Replace the EGR valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 4.

Open wire check (PG line): Open wire check (PG line) -1. Check for continuity between test point 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The PG wire is OK. Go to step 5. -NO- Repair an open in the PG wire between the EGR valve and G101, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (E_EGR line): Shorted wire check (E_EGR line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the E_EGR wire between the ECM/PCM (B2) and the EGR valve, then do the repair verification. -NO- The E_EGR wire is not shorted. Go to step 6.

Open wire check (E_EGR line): Open wire check (E_EGR line) -1. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The E_EGR wire is OK. Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open in the E_EGR wire between the ECM/PCM (B2) and the EGR valve, then do the repair verification.

EGR port and passage cleaning: EGR port and passage cleaning -1. Remove the EGR valve.  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair -2. Clean the intake manifold EGR port with throttle plate cleaner. Also, clean the passage inside the EGR valve with throttle plate cleaner. -3. Install the EGR valve.  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair -4. Reconnect all connectors. -5. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -6. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -7. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure -8. Do the EGR TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- The failure is not duplicated. Do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 8.

EGR valve check (substitution): EGR valve check (substitution) -1. Substitute a known-good EGR valve. -2. Reconnect the EGR valve 5P connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure.  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure -6. Do the EGR TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Replace the original EGR valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- The EGR valve is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Do the EGR TEST with the HDS. -7. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0404 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, EXECUTING, or OUT OF CONDITION? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then check the OBD STATUS again. -EXECUTING- Keep the test condition until a result comes on. -OUT OF CONDITION- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0406: Exhaust Gas Recirculation (EGR) Valve Position Sensor Circuit High Voltage Control System DTCs P0406

dtcs.description: EGR Valve Position Sensor Circuit High Voltage The warm up three way catalytic converter (WU-TWC) converts hydrocarbons (HC), carbon monoxide (CO), and oxides of nitrogen (NOx) in the exhaust gas to water vapor, carbon dioxide (CO2), and dinitrogen (N2). The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) fluctuates the air/fuel ratio temporarily to detect the performance of the WU-TWC and measures the degree of fluctuation in the secondary heated oxygen sensor (secondary HO2S (sensor 2)) output. If the accumulated value for a set time is too great, deterioration of the performance of the catalyst is detected, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The secondary HO2S fluctuation integration value is 3.0 V or more for at least 7.5 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check the EGR VLS in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is 4.88 V or more indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EGR valve and the ECM/PCM.

Determine possible failure area (EGR valve position sensor, others): Determine possible failure area (EGR valve position sensor, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 5.0 V? -YES- Replace the EGR valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Exhaust Gas Recirculation/EGR Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 3.

Open wire check (SG2 line): Open wire check (SG2 line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The SG2 wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the SG2 wire between the ECM/PCM (B44) and the EGR valve, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0406 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EGR valve and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0420: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.
Drive the vehicle at a speed between 45 - 75 mph (73 - 120 km/h) for at least 5 minutes, to warm up the TWC.
Drive at a steady speed between 55 - 75 mph (88 - 120 km/h) for at least 10 seconds.
Decelerate with the throttle valve fully closed.
Repeat Driving Pattern steps 3 through 4, four times.

Set a vehicle speed [VEHICLE SPEED] of 55 mph (88 km/h) on the cruise control or equivalent, and drive for at least 68 seconds. - After disconnecting the battery, extend Driving Pattern step 6 to 4 minutes or longer. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0420

dtcs.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold

EVAP Canister Purge Valve Circuit Malfunction Special Tools Required Vacuum Pump/Gauge, 0-30 inHg Snap-on YA4000A or equivalent, commercially available The fuel tank pressure (FTP) sensor is installed on the evaporative emission (EVAP) canister. The FTP sensor is used to detect leaks in the EVAP system. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) monitors the FTP sensor output voltage. The FTP sensor output voltage rises as the fuel tank pressure increases. Conversely, the FTP sensor output voltage drops as the fuel tank pressure decreases. Rapid changes in the FTP sensor output voltage due to electrical noise or an intermittent open during the EVAP leak detection may cause incorrect leak detection, so abnormal output is monitored. If the FTP sensor output voltage change occurs for specified ratio within a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The FTP sensor output voltage fluctuation occurs for more than 50 % within the set time at least 31 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0443 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EVAP canister purge valve and the ECM/PCM.

Determine possible failure area (EVAP canister purge valve stuck open failure, EVAP canister purge valve stuck closed failure): Determine possible failure area (EVAP canister purge valve stuck open failure, EVAP canister purge valve stuck closed failure) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and allow the engine to cool below 140 °F (60 °C). -2. Disconnect the vacuum hose (A) from the EVAP canister purge valve (B) in the engine compartment, then connect a vacuum pump/gauge, 0-30 inHg, to the hose. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle. Is there vacuum? -YES- The EVAP canister purge valve is stuck open. Go to step 3. -NO- The EVAP canister purge valve is stuck closed. Go to step 5.

Determine possible failure area (PC_SOL line, EVAP canister purge valve): Determine possible failure area (PC_SOL line, EVAP canister purge valve) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- There is a problem in the PC_SOL line. Go to step 4. -NO- Replace the EVAP canister purge valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Evaporative Emissions System/Canister Purge Control Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (PC_SOL line): Shorted wire check (PC_SOL line) -1. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the PC_SOL wire between the ECM/PCM (B3) and the EVAP canister purge valve, then do the repair verification. -NO- The PC_SOL wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (IG1(PCS) line): Open wire check (IG1(PCS) line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The IG1(PCS) wire is OK. Go to step 6. -NO- Repair an open in the IG1(PCS) wire between the EVAP canister purge valve and the No. B2 IG1AGS(15 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (PC_SOL line): Open wire check (PC_SOL line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The PC_SOL wire is OK. Go to step 7. -NO- Repair an open in the PC_SOL wire between the ECM/PCM (B3) and the EVAP canister purge valve, then do the repair verification.

EVAP canister purge valve internal resistance check: EVAP canister purge valve internal resistance check -1. At the purge valve side, measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 23-26 Ohms at room temperature? -YES- The EVAP canister purge valve is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the EVAP canister purge valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Evaporative Emissions System/Canister Purge Control Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0443 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, EXECUTING, or OUT OF CONDITION? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then check the OBD STATUS again. -EXECUTING- Keep idling until a result comes on. -OUT OF CONDITION- Keep idling until a result comes on. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. dtcs.description: FTP Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Problem The fuel tank pressure (FTP) sensor is installed on the evaporative emission (EVAP) canister and detects the fuel tank pressure. The FTP sensor is used to detect leaks in the EVAP system. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) monitors the FTP sensor output voltage. The FTP sensor output voltage rises as the fuel tank pressure increases. Conversely, the FTP sensor output voltage drops as the fuel tank pressure decreases. If the FTP sensor output voltage does not reach a target value for a set time after starting the engine in a cold condition, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The output from the FTP sensor [FTP SENSOR] is less than -8 kPa (-2.2 inHg, -55 mmHg) for at least 3.0 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle for 1 minute. -4. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0451 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- Replace the FTP sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Fuel Tank Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the FTP sensor and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates NOT COMPLETED, keep idling until a result comes on. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Start the engine, and let it idle for 1 minute. -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0451 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0453: Fuel Tank Pressure (FTP) Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1) Control System DTCs P0453

dtcs.description: FTP Sensor Circuit High Voltage The evaporative emission (EVAP) leak detection system uses an engine off natural vacuum (EONV) method. The EONV method detects leakage from the change in fuel tank pressure via the fuel tank pressure (FTP) sensor with the engine off. Here is an overview of the malfunction detection for the EONV method: Judgment 1: Judgment of detection of 0.09 inch leak as normal operation Judgment 2: Judgment of detection of 0.02 inch leak as normal operation Judgment 3: Detection of 0.02 inch leak [Judgments 1, 2, and 3 happen at the same time.] Judgment 1: After the engine has stopped, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) monitors the variation of the FTP sensor output to detect "no 0.09 inch leak" depending on the variation of the pressure inside the fuel tank. - If the variation of the pressure is a specified value and continues for a specified duration, it is identified as a "0.09 inch leak" and the diagnosis completes. - If the variation of the pressure is a specified value before a specified duration has passed, it is defined as "no 0.09 inch leak", the judgment of detection of a 0.09 inch leak is completed, and it goes to 0.02 inch leak monitor. Judgment 2: After the engine has stopped, the ECM/PCM monitors the variation of the FTP sensor output to detect "no 0.02 inch leak" depending on the variation corresponding to the increasing pressure inside the fuel tank. - If "no 0.02 inch leak" is detected, it is identified as normal; the diagnosis is complete. - If the pressure inside the fuel tank does not increase a specified value within a specified duration, it goes to Judgment 3. Judgment 3: The ECM/PCM continues to monitor the FTP sensor output to detect "no 0.02 inch leak" depending on the variation corresponding to the decreasing pressure inside the fuel tank until the detection completes. - If a "0.02 inch leak" is detected, it is identified as a malfunction; the diagnosis is complete. - If "no leakage" is detected, it is identified as normal; the diagnosis is complete. - If the pressure is not atmospheric pressure or less when the detection is completed, that data is stored and the diagnosis is complete.
detection_condition: The variation of pressure inside the fuel tank is 0.02 kPa (0.006 inHg, 0.15 mmHg) or less.

Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0455

dtcs.description: EVAP System Large Leak Detected

EVAP System Very Small Leak Detected Special Tools Required *Vacuum Pump/Gauge, 0-30 inHg Snap-on YA4000A or equivalent, commercially available *Vacuum/Pressure Gauge, 0-4 inHg 07JAZ-001000B NOTICE: The fuel system is designed to allow specified maximum vacuum and pressure conditions. Do not deviate from the vacuum and pressure tests as indicated in these procedures. Excessive pressure/vacuum would damage the EVAP components or cause eventual fuel tank failure. The evaporative emission (EVAP) leak detection system uses an engine off natural vacuum (EONV) method. The EONV method detects leakage from the change in fuel tank pressure via the fuel tank pressure (FTP) sensor with the engine off. Here is an overview of the malfunction detection for the EONV method: Judgment 1: Judgment of detection of 0.09 inch leak as normal operation Judgment 2: Judgment of detection of 0.02 inch leak as normal operation Judgment 3: Detection of 0.02 inch leak [Judgments 1, 2, and 3 happen at the same time.] Judgment 1: After the engine has stopped, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) monitors the variation of the FTP sensor output to detect "no 0.09 inch leak" depending on the variation of the pressure inside the fuel tank. - If the variation of the pressure is a specified value and continues for a specified duration, it is identified as a "0.09 inch leak" and the diagnosis completes. - If the variation of the pressure is a specified value before a specified duration has passed, it is defined as "no 0.09 inch leak", the judgment of detection of a 0.09 inch leak is completed, and it goes to 0.02 inch leak monitor. Judgment 2: After the engine has stopped, the ECM/PCM monitors the variation of the FTP sensor output to detect "no 0.02 inch leak" depending on the variation corresponding to the increasing pressure inside the fuel tank. - If "no 0.02 inch leak" is detected, it is identified as normal; the diagnosis is complete. - If the pressure inside the fuel tank does not increase a specified value within a specified duration, it goes to Judgment 3. Judgment 3: The ECM/PCM continues to monitor the FTP sensor output to detect "no 0.02 inch leak" depending on the variation corresponding to the decreasing pressure inside the fuel tank until the detection completes. - If a "0.02 inch leak" is detected, it is identified as a malfunction; the diagnosis is complete. - If "no leakage" is detected, it is identified as normal; the diagnosis is complete. - If the pressure is not atmospheric pressure or less when the detection is completed, that data is stored and the diagnosis is complete.
detection_condition: The change rate of the pressure rise slope inside the fuel tank is 2.8 or more.
The change rate of the pressure descending slope inside the fuel tank is 0.8 or more.
The barometric pressure is stable for at least 30 minutes.
The output from the fuel cap monitor is 0.028 or less for at least 12 seconds (when there is no NG judgment history in this drive cycle).

steps.instruction: After the vehicle has been left for an appropriate amount of time as specified, with the engine coolant temperature [ECT SENSOR 1] and intake air temperature [IAT SENSOR (2)] within the specified range, start the engine.
Drive the vehicle immediately at a speed between 25 - 75 mph (40 - 120 km/h) for at least 23 minutes.

After stopping the vehicle, turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and leave the vehicle in this condition for at least 37 minutes (EONV executes). - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle with the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine start-up within the specified temperature range, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0456

EVAP canister purge valve leak check: EVAP canister purge valve leak check -1. Disconnect the vacuum hose (purge line) (A) from the EVAP canister purge valve (B) in the engine compartment, then connect a T-fitting (C), a vacuum gauge, and a vacuum pump/gauge, 0-30 inHg, to the vacuum hose as shown. -2. Slowly apply about 2 kPa (0.6 inHg, 15 mmHg) of vacuum to the hose. Does it hold vacuum for 1 minute? -YES- Leak not detected at the EVAP canister purge valve. Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the EVAP canister purge valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Evaporative Emissions System/Canister Purge Control Valve/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Do the EVAP FUNCTION TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the FTP sensor, the EVAP canister purge valve, the EVAP canister vent shut valve, and the ECM/PCM. -NO- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 6.

Determine possible failure area (EVAP canister vent shut valve, others): Determine possible failure area (EVAP canister vent shut valve, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the fuel tank vapor recirculation tube (A) from the EVAP canister (B), and plug the EVAP canister port (C). -3. Remove the T-fitting, and connect a vacuum pump/gauge to the vacuum hose (A) as shown. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Select EVAP CVS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -6. Apply vacuum to the hose until the FTP reads 1.90 V (-0.59 inHg, -15.1 mmHg).

EVAP canister vent shut valve operation check: EVAP canister vent shut valve operation check -1. Select EVAP CVS OFF in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -2. Disconnect the fresh air hose (A) from the EVAP canister (B), and plug the EVAP canister vent shut valve ports (C). -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Select EVAP PCS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -5. Apply vacuum to the hose until the FTP reads 1.90 V (-0.59 inHg, -15.1 mmHg).

EVAP canister purge line visual check: EVAP canister purge line visual check Is the line OK? -YES- Replace these parts, then do the repair verification. * FTP sensor O-ring * EVAP canister vent shut valve case and O-ring * EVAP canister -NO- Reconnect or repair the EVAP canister purge line, then do the repair verification.

Fuel fill pipe and fuel vapor return pipe visual check: Fuel fill pipe and fuel vapor return pipe visual check -1. Select EVAP CVS OFF in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -2. Check for looseness or damage at the fuel fill pipe and the fuel vapor return pipe. Are the parts OK? -YES- The fuel fill pipe and fuel vapor return pipe are OK. Check the fuel tank unit base gasket and the fuel tank, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair or replace the damaged parts, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Install the parts in the reverse order of removal. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Do the EVAP FUNCTION TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Troubleshooting is complete. -NO- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the FTP sensor, the EVAP canister purge valve, the EVAP canister vent shut valve, and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0456: Evaporative Emission (EVAP) System Very Small Leak Detected (Engine Type: R18Z1)

dtcs.description: EVAP System Leak Detected/Fuel Fill Cap Loose or Missing The fuel level sensor is incorporated with the fuel pump and installed in the fuel tank. Using a built-in potentiometer and float, it converts the movement of the float into electrical signals that correspond to the fuel level, and it indicates the amount of fuel in the fuel tank. If the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) receives no change in the fuel level sensor output after driving for a specified number of miles, it detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The change in the fuel level sensor output [FUEL LEVEL] is 3.5 % or less.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Fuel fill cap installation check: Fuel fill cap installation check -1. Check the fuel fill cap (the cap must say TIGHTEN TO CLICK). It should turn 1/4 turn after it's tight, then it clicks. Is the correct fuel fill cap installed and properly tightened? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Replace the fuel fill cap or tighten the cap, then do the repair verification.

Fuel fill cap seal and fuel fill pipe mating surface visual check: Fuel fill cap seal and fuel fill pipe mating surface visual check -1. Check the fuel fill cap seal (A) and the fuel fill pipe mating surface (B). Verify that the fuel fill cap tether cord (C) is not caught under the cap. Is the fuel fill cap seal missing or damaged, is the fuel fill pipe damaged, or is the tether cord caught under the cap? -YES- Replace the fuel fill cap or the fuel fill pipe, then do the repair verification. -NO- The fuel fill cap and the fuel fill pipe are OK. Go to step 3.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Do the EVAP FUNCTION TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the FTP sensor, the EVAP canister vent shut valve, and the ECM/PCM. -NO- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 4.

EVAP canister vent shut valve operation check: EVAP canister vent shut valve operation check -1. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -3. Remove the EVAP canister vent shut valve from the EVAP canister  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Evaporative Emissions System/Leak Detection Solenoid/Service and Repair. -4. Connect the 2P connector to the EVAP canister vent shut valve. -5. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -6. Select EVAP CVS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -7. Check the EVAP canister vent shut valve (A) operation. Is the valve free of corrosion and does it operate properly? -YES- The EVAP canister vent shut valve is OK. Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the EVAP canister vent shut valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Evaporative Emissions System/Leak Detection Solenoid/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

EVAP canister purge line check: EVAP canister purge line check -1. Check the routing of the EVAP canister purge line. Is there any damage or blockage? -YES- Remove the blockage and replace the EVAP canister purge line if needed, then do the repair verification. -NO- Do the DTC P0455 troubleshooting  See: P0455/DTC Troubleshooting. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Install the parts in the reverse order of removal. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Do the EVAP FUNCTION TEST with the HDS. Is the result OK? -YES- Troubleshooting is complete. -NO- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the FTP sensor, the EVAP canister vent shut valve, and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0461: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Range/Performance Problem (Engine Type: R18Z1) Control System DTCs P0461

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Range/Performance Problem . The fuel level sensor (fuel gauge sending unit) is incorporated into the fuel pump and installed in the fuel tank. Using a built-in potentiometer and float, it converts the movement of the float to electrical signals as an output that corresponds fuel level variations in the fuel tank. The fuel level, which is indicated by the gauge control module (tach), is sent to the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) via the controller area network (CAN). If the ECM/PCM detects a signal from the fuel level sensor (fuel gauge sending unit) below a predetermined value for a set time, it detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The fuel gauge sensor (fuel gauge sending unit) output voltage is 0.10 V or less for at least 5 seconds.

steps.instruction: Fuel gauge sending unit performance check: Fuel gauge sending unit performance check -1. Test the fuel gauge sending unit. Is the fuel gauge sending unit OK? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the fuel gauge sending unit and the gauge control module (tach). -NO- Replace the fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Are any Pending or Confirmed DTCs indicated? -YES- Go to the indicated DTCs Troubleshooting. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0462: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Low Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1) Control System DTCs P0462

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Low Voltage The fuel level sensor (fuel gauge sending unit) is incorporated into the fuel pump and installed in the fuel tank. Using a built-in potentiometer and float, it converts the movement of the float to electrical signals as an output that corresponds fuel level variations in the fuel tank. The fuel level, which is indicated by the gauge control module (tach), is sent to the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) via the controller area network (CAN). If the ECM/PCM detects a signal from the fuel level sensor (fuel gauge sending unit) above a predetermined value for a set time, it detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The fuel gauge sensor (fuel gauge sending unit) output voltage is 4.92 V or more for at least 5 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Remove the access panel from the floor. -3. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -4. Wait 5 seconds. -5. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0462 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel gauge sending unit.

Fuel gauge sending unit check: Fuel gauge sending unit check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Wait 5 seconds. -5. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0463 indicated? -YES- Replace the fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. -NO- The fuel gauge sending unit is OK. Go to step 3.

Determine possible failure area (FUEL_GAGE+ line, others): Determine possible failure area (FUEL_GAGE+ line, others) -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Go to step 4.

Shorted wire check (FUEL_GAGE+ line): Shorted wire check (FUEL_GAGE+ line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Remove the gauge control module (tach).  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair/Gauge Control Module (Tach) Removal and Installation -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the FUEL_GAGE+ wire between the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel tank unit, then do the repair verification. -NO- The FUEL_GAGE+ wire is OK. Replace the gauge control module (tach)  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair/Gauge Control Module (Tach) Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification.

Gauge control module (tach) check: Gauge control module (tach) check -1. Reconnect the following connector. -2. Remove the fuel tank unit.  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Tank Unit Removal/Installation -3. Connect the following connector. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -6. Set the float (A) to the E position. -7. Check the fuel gauge. Does the gauge move to the empty position? -YES- The gauge control module (tach) is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the gauge control module (tach)  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair/Gauge Control Module (Tach) Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reinstall all removed parts in the reverse order of removal. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0462 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel gauge sending unit. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0463: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit High Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1) Control System DTCs P0463

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit High Voltage The fuel vapor in the fuel tank is temporarily stored in the evaporative emission (EVAP) canister and drawn into the engine through the EVAP canister purge valve. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) controls the amount of vapor introduced into the engine by varying the duty cycle of the EVAP canister purge valve according to the condition of the engine. [STEP 1] The EVAP canister purge valve opens normally (EVAP canister purge valve OPEN OK) and detects that the purge flow is normal when the pulse of the EVAP canister purge valve duty cycle is transmitted to the fuel tank pressure (FTP) sensor during purge flow (pulse method). OK determination: Pulse exists (P145C OK) -

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Wait 5 seconds. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0463 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel gauge sending unit.

Determine possible failure area (FUEL_GAGE+ line or FUEL_GAGE- line, others): Determine possible failure area (FUEL_GAGE+ line or FUEL_GAGE-, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Remove the access panel from the floor. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Go to step 3.

Open wire check (FUEL_GAGE- line): Open wire check (FUEL_GAGE- line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -3. Remove the gauge control module (tach).  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair/Gauge Control Module (Tach) Removal and Installation -4. Disconnect the following connector. -5. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The FUEL_GAGE- wire is OK. Go to step 4. -NO- Repair an open in the FUEL_GAGE- wire between the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel tank unit, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (FUEL_GAGE+ line): Open wire check (FUEL_GAGE+ line) -1. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The FUEL_GAGE+ wire is OK. Go to step 5. -NO- Repair an open in the FUEL_GAGE+ wire between the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel tank unit, then do the repair verification.

Fuel gauge sending unit performance check: Fuel gauge sending unit performance check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Remove the jumper wire from the fuel tank unit 4P connector. -3. Remove the fuel tank unit.  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Tank Unit Removal/Installation -4. Test the fuel gauge sending unit.  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Tank Unit/Testing and Inspection Is the fuel gauge sending unit OK? -YES- The fuel gauge sending unit is OK. Go to step 6. -NO- Repair the fuel gauge sending unit, then do the repair verification.

Gauge control module (tach) check: Gauge control module (tach) check -1. Connect the following connectors. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -4. Set the float (A) to the F position. -5. Check the fuel gauge. Does the gauge move to the full position? -YES- The gauge control module (tach) is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace the gauge control module (tach)  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair/Gauge Control Module (Tach) Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reinstall all removed parts in the reverse order of removal. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0463 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the gauge control module (tach) and the fuel gauge sending unit. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0496: Evaporative Emission (EVAP) System High Purge Flow Detected (Engine Type: R18Z1)

dtcs.description: EVAP Canister Vent Shut Valve Circuit Low Voltage The evaporative emission (EVAP) canister vent shut valve is attached to the EVAP canister to control the venting of the EVAP canister to atmosphere. The EVAP canister vent shut valve is open (open to atmosphere) when the VSV signal is OFF. If the return signal is "ON" when the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) outputs the "OFF" signal to the EVAP canister vent shut valve, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The return signal is "ON" for at least 5 seconds when the ECM/PCM outputs the "OFF" signal to the EVAP canister vent shut valve.

steps.instruction: EVAP system check: EVAP system check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0498 indicated? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Go to step 2.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Select EVAP CVS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -2. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0498 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 3. -NO- The EVAP system is OK. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EVAP canister vent shut valve and the ECM/PCM.

Open wire check (FI_SUB_RLY_OUT line): Open wire check (FI_SUB_RLY_OUT line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- The FI_SUB_RLY_OUT wire is OK. Go to step 4. -NO- Repair an open in the FI_SUB_RLY_OUT wire between the EVAP canister vent shut valve and the PGM-FI subrelay, then do the repair verification.

EVAP canister purge valve internal resistance check: EVAP canister purge valve internal resistance check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. At the vent shut valve side, measure the resistance between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 25 -30 Ohms at room temperature? -YES- The EVAP canister vent shut valve is OK. Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the EVAP canister vent shut valve  See: Powertrain Management/Emission Control Systems/Evaporative Emissions System/Leak Detection Solenoid/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (VSV line): Shorted wire check (VSV line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the VSV wire between the EVAP canister vent shut valve and the ECM/PCM (A32), then do the repair verification. -NO- The VSV wire is not shorted. Go to step 6.
