Open wire check (VSV line): Open wire check (VSV line) -1. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The VSV wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the VSV wire between the EVAP canister vent shut valve and the ECM/PCM (A32), then do the repair verification. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Select EVAP CVS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -7. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0498 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then check the OBD STATUS again. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0499: Evaporative Emission (EVAP) Canister Vent Shut Valve Circuit High Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1) Control System DTCs P0499

dtcs.description: EVAP Canister Vent Shut Valve Circuit High Voltage A target idle speed that meets the engine operating conditions (coolant temperature, A/C ON or OFF, etc.) is stored in the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The ECM/PCM monitors and controls the idle speed so that the actual idle speed is equal to the target idle speed. If the actual idle speed varies beyond a specified value from the target speed over a certain period of time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction in the idle speed control system and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The actual idle speed is 100 rpm less than the target idle speed for at least 24 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Select EVAP CVS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0499 indicated? -YES- Failure is duplicated. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the EVAP canister vent shut valve and the ECM/PCM. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Select EVAP CVS ON in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0499 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then check the OBD STATUS again. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0506: Idle Control System RPM Lower Than Expected (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9)
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.

Let the engine idle for at least 24 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0506

dtcs.description: Idle Control System RPM Lower Than Expected A target idle speed that meets the engine operating conditions (coolant temperature, A/C ON or OFF, etc.) is stored in the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The ECM/PCM monitors and controls the idle speed so that the actual idle speed is equal to the target idle speed. If the actual idle speed varies beyond a specified value from the target speed over a certain period of time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction in the idle speed control system and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The actual idle speed is 200 rpm greater than the target idle speed for at least 24 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Check for these conditions in the DATA LIST with the HDS: * ECT SENSOR 1 above 158 °F (70 °C) * IAT SENSOR above 32 °F (0 °C) * VEHICLE SPEED is 0 mph (0 km/h) * ST FUEL TRIM between 0.69 and 1.47 * FSS is CLOSED -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0506 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, go to step 3. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep idling until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 1-4 and recheck.

Throttle body visual check: Throttle body visual check -1. Remove the intake air duct from the throttle body.  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement -2. Check for dirt, carbon, or damage in the throttle bore. Is there dirt, carbon, or damage in the throttle bore? -YES- If there is dirt or carbon, clean the throttle body  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Procedures/Throttle Body Cleaning. Also check for damage to the air cleaner element, then do the repair verification. If there is damage in the throttle bore, replace the throttle body  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement, then do the repair verification. -NO- The throttle body is OK. Check the A/C system, then go to step 4.

Throttle body visual check: Throttle body visual check -1. Remove the intake air duct from the throttle body.  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement -2. Check for dirt, carbon, or damage in the throttle bore. Is there dirt, carbon, or damage in the throttle bore? -YES- If there is dirt or carbon, clean the throttle body  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Procedures/Throttle Body Cleaning. Also check for damage to the air cleaner element, then do the repair verification. If there is damage in the throttle bore, replace the throttle body  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Throttle Body/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement, then do the repair verification. -NO- The throttle body is OK. Go to step 4.

Idle control system check: Idle control system check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Check for these conditions in the DATA LIST with the HDS: * ECT SENSOR 1 above 158 °F (70 °C) * IAT SENSOR above 32 °F (0 °C) * VEHICLE SPEED is 0 mph (0 km/h) * ST FUEL TRIM between 0.69 and 1.47 * FSS is CLOSED
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed [ENGINE SPEED] at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.

Let the engine idle for at least 24 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0507

dtcs.description: Idle Control System RPM Higher Than Expected The A/C pressure sensor measures the change in the pressure of the air conditioning refrigerant. The signal from the A/C pressure sensor is low voltage at low pressure (when the air conditioning load is low) and high voltage at high pressure (when the air conditioning load is high). The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) compares the expected voltage to the A/C pressure sensor output voltage. When the A/C pressure sensor output voltage is lower than the expected voltage for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The A/C pressure sensor output voltage [A/C PRESSURE SENSOR] is 0.24 V or less for at least 10 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -4. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0507 with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep idling until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, recheck with different load conditions (electrical, A/C, gear position, etc.), then go to step 1-3.

Vacuum leak check: Vacuum leak check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Check for vacuum leaks at these parts: * PCV valve * PCV hose * EVAP canister purge valve * Throttle body * Intake manifold * Brake booster and hose Are there any leaks? -YES- Repair or replace the leaking part(s), then do the repair verification. -NO- Recheck with different load conditions (turn on the headlights, blower motor, or A/C; change the gear position, etc.), then do the repair verification. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -3. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -4. Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (A/T in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle. -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P0507 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, EXECUTING, or OUT OF CONDITION? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. -EXECUTING- Keep the test condition until a result comes on. -OUT OF CONDITION- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0532: A/C Pressure Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0532

dtcs.description: A/C Pressure Sensor Circuit Low Voltage The A/C pressure sensor measures the change in the pressure of the air conditioning refrigerant. The signal from the A/C pressure sensor is low voltage at low pressure (when the air conditioning load is low) and high voltage at high pressure (when the air conditioning load is high). The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) compares the expected voltage to the A/C pressure sensor output voltage. When the A/C pressure sensor output voltage is higher than the expected voltage for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The A/C pressure sensor output voltage [A/C PRESSURE SENSOR] is 4.76 V or more for at least 10 seconds.
[ ]: HDS Parameter

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Start the engine, and let it idle. -2. Turn the blower switch on. -3. Turn the A/C switch on. -4. Check the A/C PRESSURE SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 0.24 V or less? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/C pressure sensor and the ECM/PCM.

Determine possible failure area (PD_SENSOR line, others): Determine possible failure area (PD_SENSOR line, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Check the A/C PRESSURE SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 0.24 V or less? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Go to step 3.

Determine possible failure area (A/C pressure sensor, others): Determine possible failure area (A/C pressure sensor, others) -1. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 5.0 V? -YES- Replace the A/C pressure sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Hose/Line HVAC/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Go to step 4.

Open wire check (VCC6 line): Open wire check (VCC6 line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The VCC6 wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the VCC6 wire between the ECM/PCM (A30) and the A/C pressure sensor, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (PD_SENSOR line): Shorted wire check (PD_SENSOR line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the PD_SENSOR wire between the ECM/PCM (A27) and the A/C pressure sensor, then do the repair verification. -NO- The PD_SENSOR wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Start the engine, and let it idle. -7. Turn the blower switch on. -8. Turn the A/C switch on. -9. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0532 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/C pressure sensor and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0533: A/C Pressure Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0533

dtcs.description: A/C Pressure Sensor Circuit High Voltage The alternator is driven by the engine, and it generates electricity according to the commands from the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM). The LIN line is connected to the alternator and transmits/receives information to/ from the ECM/PCM. The ECM/PCM sets a target generating voltage in the range of 12.5 V to 14.5 V and commands the alternator via the LIN line. If the FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT terminal voltage is a set value for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT terminal voltage is 11.0 V or less for at least 60 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Start the engine, and let it idle. -2. Turn the blower switch on. -3. Turn the A/C switch on. -4. Check the A/C PRESSURE SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 4.75 V or more? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/C pressure sensor and the ECM/PCM.

Determine possible failure area (A/C pressure sensor, others): Determine possible failure area (A/C pressure sensor, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Check the A/C PRESSURE SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there about 4.75 V or more? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Replace the A/C pressure sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Hose/Line HVAC/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Determine possible failure area (PD_SENSOR line, SG6 line): Determine possible failure area (PD_SENSOR line, SG6 line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Remove the jumper wire. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there about 5.0 V? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 5.

Open wire check (PD_SENSOR line): Open wire check (PD_SENSOR line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The PD_SENSOR wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the PD_SENSOR wire between the ECM/PCM (A27) and the A/C pressure sensor, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (SG6 line): Open wire check (SG6 line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The SG6 wire is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the SG6 wire between the ECM/PCM (A10) and the A/C pressure sensor, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Start the engine, and let it idle. -7. Turn the blower switch on. -8. Turn the A/C switch on. -9. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0533 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/C pressure sensor and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0562: Charging System Low Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0562

dtcs.description: Charging System Low Voltage If there is a short to ground in the harness between the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) and PGM-FI main relay 1, PGM-FI main relay 1 stays ON even if the ignition switch is turned to LOCK (0), and the ECM/PCM remains active. However, the engine is not running because power for the gauges, the ignition, and the fuel pump is turned OFF by the ignition switch. When the ECM/PCM operates for a set time after the ignition switch is turned to LOCK (0), it detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ECM/PCM operates for at least 5 seconds after the ignition switch is turned to LOCK (0).

steps.instruction: DTC check: DTC check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle without load for 1 minute. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16BB indicated? -YES- Do the troubleshooting for DTC P16BB  See: P16BB/DTC Troubleshooting. -NO- Go to step 2.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0562 is indicated? -YES- Repair or replace the alternator  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Alternator/Service and Repair/Overhaul. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the +B terminal on the alternator and the under-hood fuse/relay box. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0563: Engine Control Module (ECM)/Powertrain Control Module (PCM) Power Source Circuit Unexpected Voltage (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0563

dtcs.description: ECM/PCM Power Source Circuit Unexpected Voltage The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) is equipped with an update program to update its control program. The programs in the CPU of the ECM/PCM are classified as an ECM/PCM program (update-capable program) and a program for the update function (non-updatable program). The program update only updates the ECM/PCM program. When the ECM/PCM power is turned off during an update, the power for the update function is lost, and the update process stops. When the program update is stopped before it is completed, the ECM/PCM stores a DTC that indicates the update is not finished.
detection_condition: The ECM/PCM program update stops 1 second before it is finished.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -4. Wait 10 seconds. -5. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0563 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at PGM-FI main relay 1 and the ECM/PCM.

Determine possible failure area (FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT line, others): Determine possible failure area (FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT line, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Go to step 3.

Shorted wire check (FI_MAIN_RLY_CL- line): Shorted wire check (FI_MAIN_RLY_CL- line) -1. Remove PGM-FI main relay 1 (A) from the under-dash fuse/relay box. -2. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the FI_MAIN_RLY_CL- wire between the ECM/PCM (A7) and PGM-FI main relay 1, then do the repair verification. -NO- The FI_MAIN_RLY_CL- wire is OK. Go to step 4.

Relay check: Relay check -1. Test PGM-FI main relay 1  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is PGM-FI main relay 1 OK? -YES- Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace PGM-FI main relay 1, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT line to power): Shorted wire check (FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT line to power) -1. Remove PGM-FI main relay 1 (A) from the under-dash fuse/relay box. -2. Measure the voltage between test points 1 and 2. Is there battery voltage? -YES- Repair a short to power in the FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT wire between the ECM/PCM (A9) and PGM-FI main relay 1, then do the repair verification. -NO- The FI_MAIN_RLY_OUT wire is OK. Go to step 6.

Relay check: Relay check -1. Test PGM-FI main relay 1  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is PGM-FI main relay 1 OK? -YES- PGM-FI main relay 1 is OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Replace PGM-FI main relay 1, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -7. Wait 10 seconds. -8. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -9. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0563 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at PGM-FI main relay 1 and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0602: Engine Control Module (ECM)/Powertrain Control Module (PCM) Programming Error (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0602

dtcs.description: ECM/PCM Programming Error The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) stores a vehicle identification number (VIN) in the keep alive memory (KAM) and outputs the VIN according to commands from the HDS. The VIN for each vehicle is registered to the ECM/PCM using the HDS. The registered VIN is read by the CPU from the KAM after the ignition switch is turned to ON (II), or after the Clear command is executed. If the VIN is not registered in the KAM when the ignition switch is turned to ON (II), or when the Clear command is executed, the ECM/PCM detects a VIN unregistered condition and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The VIN is not registered in the KAM in the ECM/PCM.

steps.instruction: Problem verification (update): Problem verification (update) -1. Do the ECM/PCM update procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update. -2. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0602 indicated? -YES- Replace the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. -NO- Update is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0630: VIN Not Programmed Or Mismatch (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0630

dtcs.description: VIN Not Programmed or Mismatch The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) is equipped with a voltage monitor (5 V power source) to supply a stable 5 V to each sensor as reference voltage. The correct voltage for the sensors are loaded in the CPU of the ECM/PCM (A/D input) and when the sensor power voltage is a set value (high or low) for a certain time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The sensor power voltage is 5.2 V or more, or 4.5 V or less, for at least 2.0 seconds.

steps.instruction: VIN check: VIN check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check the VIN with the HDS. Does the HDS show the vehicle's VIN? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 2.
VIN check (rewrite): VIN check (rewrite) -1. Input the VIN to the ECM/PCM with the HDS. Does the HDS show COMPLETE? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 3.
DTC check: DTC check -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P062F indicated? -YES- Go to the DTC P062F troubleshooting  See: P062F/DTC Troubleshooting. -NO- Go to step 4.

ECM/PCM check: ECM/PCM check -1. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and wait 5 seconds. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0630 indicated? -YES- Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's . Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0641: Sensor Reference Voltage A Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0641

dtcs.description: Sensor Reference Voltage A Malfunction The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) is equipped with a voltage monitor (5 V power source) to supply a stable 5 V to each sensor as reference voltage. The correct voltage for the sensors are loaded in the CPU of the ECM/PCM (A/D input) and when the sensor power voltage is a set value (high or low) for a certain time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The sensor power voltage is 5.2 V or more, or 4.5 V or less, for at least 2.0 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0641 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM and these parts: * MAP sensor * Output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor * Throttle body * APP sensor

Determine possible failure area (VCC lines, others): Determine possible failure area (VCC lines, others) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the connectors from these parts: * MAP sensor * Output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor * Throttle body * APP sensor -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -5. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0641 indicated? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 3.

Shorted part check: Shorted part check -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS while reconnecting these parts, one at a time: * MAP sensor * Output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor * Throttle body * APP sensor Is DTC P0641 indicated? -YES- Replace the part that caused the DTC when it was reconnected, then do the repair verification. -NO- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM and the following parts, then go to step 1. * MAP sensor * Output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor * Throttle body * APP sensor

Shorted wire check (VCC lines): Shorted wire check (VCC lines) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connectors. -4. Check for continuity between these test points and body ground individually. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the VCC wire between the ECM/PCM (A36, C22, or C23) and each part that was disconnected in step 2-2, then do the repair verification. -NO- The VCC wires are OK. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0641 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM and the following parts, then go to step 1. * MAP sensor * Output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor * Throttle body * APP sensor -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0651: Sensor Reference Voltage B Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0651

dtcs.description: Sensor Reference Voltage B Malfunction After the ignition switch is turned to LOCK (0), the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) does not shut down immediately. After finishing a predetermined process according to the request of each device and system, the power supply is automatically disconnected (self shut-down function). The ECM/PCM power is disconnected by controlling PGM-FI main relay 1. During a normal ECM/PCM shut down, the shut down process is executed by the CPU, PGM-FI main relay 1 is turned off, and the voltage to the ECM/PCM is turned off to shut down the ECM/PCM. When the voltage to the ECM/PCM is turned off and the ECM/PCM shuts down without the normal shut down procedure, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction in the PGM-FI main relay 1 control circuit and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ECM/PCM is shut down within 1 second without the normal shut down procedure.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0651 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM and these parts: * EGR valve * Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor * APP sensor * A/C pressure sensor * FTP sensor

Determine possible failure area (VCC lines, others): Determine possible failure area (VCC lines, others): -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the connectors from these parts: * EGR valve * Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor * APP sensor * A/C pressure sensor * FTP sensor -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -5. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0651 indicated? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Go to step 3.

Shorted part check: Shorted part check -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS while reconnecting these parts, one at a time: * EGR valve * Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor * APP sensor * A/C pressure sensor * FTP sensor Is DTC P0651 indicated? -YES- Replace the part that caused the DTC when it was reconnected, then do the repair verification. -NO- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM and the following parts, then go to step 1. * EGR valve * Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor * APP sensor * A/C pressure sensor * FTP sensor

Shorted wire check (VCC lines): Shorted wire check (VCC lines) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connectors. -4. Check for continuity between these test points and body ground individually. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short in the VCC wire between the ECM/PCM (A30, A35, or B29) and each part that was disconnected in step 2, then do the repair verification. -NO- Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification: -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Reconnect all connectors. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Troubleshoot The Fuel And Emissions Systems. -5. Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure. -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0651 indicated? -YES- Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM/PCM and the following parts, then go to step 1. * EGR valve * Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor * APP sensor * A/C pressure sensor * FTP sensor -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0685: Engine Control Module (ECM)/Powertrain Control Module (PCM) Power Control Circuit/Internal Circuit Malfunction (Engine Type: R18Z1, R18A9) Control System DTCs P0685

dtcs.description: ECM/PCM Power Control Circuit/Internal Circuit Malfunction The transmission range switch is attached to a control shaft. Operating the shift lever makes the control shaft rotate via the shift cable. The A/T gear position indicator shows which position is selected according to the Low/High signal combinations, which vary based on the control shaft rotational angle. The control shaft changes the position of the transmission range switch, activates the manual valve, and switches hydraulic pressure to shift the transmission through forward/neutral/reverse. The transmission range switch signal is used to determine the shift schedule. The voltage is high (5 V or 12 V) at the powertrain control module (PCM) input terminal when each transmission range switch position is open, and it is low (0 V) when each switch is closed. If the PCM detects multiple switch inputs instead of the correct switch input for the selected range at that time, it detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: One of these conditions occurs.
The PCM detects the selected range switch input and another range switch input (except FWD switch input [FORWARD SWITCH (ATPFWD)])
simultaneously for at least 1 second.
The PCM detects the P, R, or N range switch input [A/T P, R, or N SWITCH] and the FWD switch input [FORWARD SWITCH (ATPFWD)]
simultaneously for at least 1 second.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and let it idle for 30 seconds. -4. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -5. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -6. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0685 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Update the ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update if it does not have the latest software, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Intermittent failure. Check the following items: * PGM-FI main relay 1 * PGM-FI main fuse * Poor connections or loose terminals between the ECM/PCM and the under-hood fuse/relay box * Poor or loose ECM/PCM ground terminals and connections * Poor or loose battery cables and connections * Check the battery Repair verification: -1. Start the engine, and let it idle for 30 seconds. -2. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0685 indicated? -YES- If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0705: Transmission Range Switch (Multiple Shift - Position Input)
Start the engine.

With the brake pedal pressed, place the shift lever in each position for at least 1 second. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0705

dtcs.description: Transmission Range Switch (Multiple Shift - Position Input) The transmission range switch is attached to a control shaft. Operating the shift lever makes the control shaft rotate via the shift cable. The A/T gear position indicator shows which position is selected according to the Low/High signal combinations, which vary based on the control shaft rotational angle. The control shaft changes the position of the transmission range switch, activates the manual valve, and switches hydraulic pressure to shift the transmission through forward/neutral/reverse. The transmission range switch signal is used to determine the shift schedule. The voltage is high (5 V or 12 V) at the powertrain control module (PCM) input terminal when each transmission range switch position is open, and it is low (0 V) when each switch is closed. If the FWD switch stays open while the vehicle repeatedly accelerates to a specified vehicle speed and then stops despite being in D or D3, the PCM detects a malfunction in the transmission range switch (open) and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: No FWD position signal [FORWARD SWITCH (ATPFWD)] is detected when the vehicle speed [VEHICLE SPEED] changes from 6 mph (10 km/h)
--> 25 mph (40 km/h) --> 6 mph (10 km/h) in D or D3.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Compare the ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3/S, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, and ATP_RVS inputs with the HDS to the following table, in each shift lever position. o: ON / x: OFF Do the transmission range switch signals match? -YES- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. -NO- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2.

Transmission range switch signal check: Transmission range switch signal check -1. Compare the ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3/S, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, and ATP_RVS inputs with the HDS to the following table, in each shift lever position. o: ON / x: OFF Are any transmission range switch signals ON in all shift lever positions? -YES- Go to step 4. -NO- Record the abnormal circuit, then go to step 3.

Transmission range switch check 1: Transmission range switch check 1 -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Connect the transmission range switch connector terminal that incorrectly indicates ON in step 2 to ground with a jumper wire. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Compare the ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3/S, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, and ATP_RVS inputs with the HDS to the following table. o: ON / x: OFF Do multiple transmission range switch signals indicate ON? -YES- The transmission range switch is OK. Repair a short in the wires between the corresponding transmission range switch connector terminal and the PCM. Refer to the following table. -NO- Replace the transmission range switch  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Computers and Control Systems/Transmission Position Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair.

Transmission range switch check 2: Transmission range switch check 2 -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Check the transmission range switch signal that remained ON with the HDS to the following table. o: ON / x: OFF Do any transmission range switch signals remain ON? -YES- The transmission range switch is OK. Go to step 5. -NO- Replace the transmission range switch  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Computers and Control Systems/Transmission Position Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair.

Shorted wire check (ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, ATP_RVS line): Shorted wire check corresponding wire -1. Check for continuity to ground in the circuit that remained ON in step 4. Does the circuit that indicated ON have continuity to ground? -YES- Repair a short to ground in the wire between the corresponding transmission range switch connector terminal and the PCM. -NO- The wires are OK. Update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update. If the PCM was updated, replace the PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0706: Transmission Range Switch (Open)
Start the engine.
Accelerate the vehicle in D to 25 mph (40 km/h) or more.
Slow down to 6 mph (10 km/h) for several seconds.
Stop the vehicle, and turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Repeat Driving Pattern steps 1 through 3. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0706

dtcs.description: Transmission Range Switch (Open) The ATF temperature sensor is a thermistor type sensor that changes resistance according to the change in ATF temperature. The powertrain control module (PCM) sends a 5 V reference voltage to the sensor through a pull-up resistor. When the ATF temperature is low, the sensor resistance increases and the PCM detects a high signal voltage. As the ATF temperature rises, the sensor resistance decreases and the PCM detects a low signal voltage. If the ATF temperature sensor signal does not change, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The current ATF temperature [ATF TEMPERATURE] is 14 °F (-10 °C) or less after Enable Conditions.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Make sure the shift cable is properly adjusted. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Compare the ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3/S, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, and ATP_RVS inputs with the HDS to the following table, in each shift lever position. o: ON / x: OFF Do the transmission range switch signals match? -YES- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. -NO- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2.

Transmission range switch signal check: Transmission range switch signal check -1. Compare the ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3/S, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, and ATP_RVS inputs with the HDS to the following table, in each shift lever position. o: ON / x: OFF Do all signals remain OFF? -YES- Go to step 5. -NO- Record the abnormal circuit, then go to step 3.

Transmission range switch check: Transmission range switch check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Connect the transmission range switch connector terminal that did not indicate ON in step 2 to ground with a jumper wire. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Check the transmission range switch signal that did not indicate ON with the HDS. Does the transmission range switch indicate ON? -YES- Replace the transmission range switch  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Computers and Control Systems/Transmission Position Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair. -NO- The transmission range switch is OK. Go to step 4.

Open wire check (ATP_P, ATP_R, ATP_N, ATP_D, ATP_D3, ATP_2-1, ATP_FWD, ATP_RVS line): Open wire check in corresponding wire -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between the transmission range switch connector terminal and the PCM terminal of the input which indicated OFF. Refer to the following table. Is there continuity? -YES- The wire(s) is OK. Update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update. If the PCM was updated, replace the PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. -NO- Repair an open in the wire between the corresponding transmission range switch connector terminal and the PCM.

Open wire check (GND line): Open wire check (GND line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The GND wire is OK, replace the transmission range switch  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Computers and Control Systems/Transmission Position Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair. -NO- Repair an open in the GND wire between the transmission range switch and ground (G101), or repair poor ground (G101). Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0711: ATF Temperature Sensor (Range/Performance)
Start the engine with the engine coolant temperature [ENGINE COOLANT TEMPERATURE] at 95 °F (35 °C) or less.
Warm up the engine until the engine coolant temperature [ENGINE COOLANT TEMPERATURE] reaches 158 °F (70 °C) or more.

Drive the vehicle at a speed of 19 mph (30 km/h) or more with the throttle position at 4° or more for at least 5 minutes. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0711

dtcs.description: ATF Temperature Sensor (Range/Performance) The ATF temperature sensor is a thermistor type sensor that changes resistance according to the change in ATF temperature. The powertrain control module (PCM) sends a 5 V reference voltage to the sensor through a pull-up resistor. When the ATF temperature is low, the sensor resistance increases and the PCM detects a high signal voltage. As the ATF temperature rises, the sensor resistance decreases and the PCM detects a low signal voltage. When the ATF temperature sensor signal voltage to the PCM is under the specification, indicating that the temperature is above the specification (a short to ground), the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ATF temperature sensor output voltage [ATF TEMP SENSOR (V)] is less than 0.07 V for at least 10 seconds.

steps.instruction: Current ATF temperature check: Current ATF temperature check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check the ATF Temperature with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temperature indicate 14 °F (-10 °C) or below? -YES- Go to step 2. -NO- Go to step 3.

ATF temperature sensor check (after warm up): ATF temperature sensor check (after warm up) -1. Start the engine, and warm it up to normal operating temperature (the radiator fan comes on). -2. Check the ATF Temperature with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temperature remain 14 °F (-10 °C) or below? -14 °F (-10 °C) or below- Replace the ATF temperature sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -Abnormal temperature rise- If there is an abnormal temperature rise in temperature of the ATF temperature sensor, update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -14 °F (-10 °C) or higher- The ATF temperature sensor is OK. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Check the ATF Temperature with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temperature exceed 230 °F (110 °C)? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 4. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

ATF temperature sensor check (after cool down): ATF temperature sensor check (after cool down) -1. Let the engine cool until the Engine Coolant Temperature reads 122 °F (50 °C) or below with the HDS in the A/T Data List. -2. Check the ATF Temperature with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temperature remain 230 °F (110 °C) or higher? -230 °F (110 °C) or higher- Replace the ATF temperature sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -Abnormal temperature decrease- If there is an abnormal temperature decrease in temperature of the ATF temperature sensor, update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -230 °F (110 °C) or below- The ATF temperature sensor is OK. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Start the engine, and warm the engine up to normal operating temperature (the radiator fan comes on). -4. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and allow the engine coolant to cool down to the outside air temperature. -5. Block the rear wheels and raise the front of the vehicle, or raise the vehicle on a lift. Make sure it is securely supported, and allow the front wheels to rotate freely. -6. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and wait for 20 seconds, then start the engine. Warm the engine up to normal operating temperature (the radiator fan comes on). Start off in D, accelerate with the throttle opened at least 4 degrees, and run the vehicle at speeds over 19 mph (30 km/h) for at least 5 minutes. Or test-drive the vehicle for at least 20 seconds while the ATF temperature reads 14 °F to 230 °F (-10 °C to 110 °C) by monitoring with the HDS. Slow down, and stop the wheels. -7. Monitor the OBD STATUS for P0711 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the PCM was substituted, replace the original PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Check for other DTC after this troubleshooting. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the PCM was updated, substitute a known-good PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, and check the OBD STATUS again. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0712: ATF Temperature Sensor (Short)
Start the engine.

Shift to P, and wait for at least 10 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0712

dtcs.description: ATF Temperature Sensor (Short) The ATF temperature sensor is a thermistor type sensor that changes resistance according to the change in ATF temperature. The powertrain control module (PCM) sends a 5 V reference voltage to the sensor through a pull-up resistor. When the ATF temperature is low, the sensor resistance increases and the PCM detects a high signal voltage. As the ATF temperature rises, the sensor resistance decreases and the PCM detects a low signal voltage. When the ATF temperature sensor signal voltage to the PCM is above the specification, indicating that the temperature is under the specification (open), the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ATF temperature sensor output voltage [ATF TEMP SENSOR (V)] is more than 4.93 V for at least 10 seconds.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check the ATF Temp Sensor (V) with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Is the ATF Temp Sensor (V) 0.07 V or less? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for an intermittent short to ground in the TATF wire between the ATF temperature sensor and the PCM.

ATF temperature sensor check: ATF temperature sensor check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -4. Check the ATF Temp Sensor (V) with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Is the ATF Temp Sensor (V) 0.07 V or less? -YES- The ATF temperature sensor is OK. Go to step 3. -NO- Replace the ATF temperature sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification.

Shorted wire check (TATF line): Shorted wire check (TATF line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- Repair a short to ground in the TATF wire between the shift solenoid wire harness and the PCM, then do the repair verification. -NO- The TATF wire is OK. Update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Reconnect all connectors. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -4. Start the engine with the shift lever in P, and wait for at least 20 seconds. -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for P0712 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the PCM was substituted, replace the original PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Check for other DTC after this troubleshooting. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the PCM was updated, substitute a known-good PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, and check the OBD STATUS again. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0713: ATF Temperature Sensor (Open)
Start the engine.

Shift to P, and wait for at least 10 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0713

dtcs.description: ATF Temperature Sensor (Open) The ATF temperature sensor is a thermistor type sensor that changes resistance according to the change in ATF temperature. The powertrain control module (PCM) sends a 5 V reference voltage to the sensor through a pull-up resistor. When the ATF temperature is low, the sensor resistance increases and the PCM detects a high signal voltage. As the ATF temperature rises, the sensor resistance decreases and the PCM detects a low signal voltage. When the engine is stopped and enough time has passed, the ATF temperature will equalize to the engine coolant temperature. When the temperature in the ATF temperature sensor deviates from the temperature in engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensor 2 after the engine is stopped and enough time has passed, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The temperature in the ATF temperature sensor deviates from the temperature in ECT sensor 2, less than -43 °F (-24 °C) or greater than 57 °F (32 °C)*.
*: Greater than 108 °F (60 °C) if engine block heater is equipped.

steps.instruction: Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check the ATF Temp Sensor (V) with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temp Sensor (V) exceed 4.93 V? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 2. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals between the ATF temperature sensor and the PCM.

Determine possible failure area (ATF temperature sensor, SG2 line, TATF line): Determine possible failure area (ATF temperature sensor, SG2 line, TATF line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Disconnect the following connector. -3. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Check the ATF Temp Sensor (V) with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temp Sensor (V) drop to 0.07 V or below? -YES- Go to step 3. -NO- Go to step 4.

ATF temperature sensor check: ATF temperature sensor check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Remove the jumper wire. -3. Connect terminals A and B with a jumper wire. -4. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -5. Check the ATF Temp Sensor (V) with the HDS in the A/T Data List. Does the ATF Temp Sensor (V) remain 0.07 V or below? -YES- Replace the ATF temperature sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- The ATF temperature sensor is OK. Repair an open in the SG2 wire between the shift solenoid wire harness and the PCM, then do the repair verification.

Open wire check (TATF line): Open wire check (TATF line) -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0). -2. Jump the SCS line with the HDS. -3. Disconnect the following connector. -4. Check for continuity between test points 1 and 2. Is there continuity? -YES- The TATF wire is OK. Update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good PCM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair an open in the TATF wire between the shift solenoid wire harness and the PCM, then do the repair verification. Repair verification (OBD STATUS check): -1. Reconnect all connectors. -2. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -3. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -4. Start the engine with the shift lever in P, and wait for at least 20 seconds. -5. Monitor the OBD STATUS for P0713 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED, FAILED, or NOT COMPLETED? -PASSED- Troubleshooting is complete. If the PCM was substituted, replace the original PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Check for other DTC after this troubleshooting. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. -FAILED- Go to step 1 and recheck. If the PCM was updated, substitute a known-good PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, and check the OBD STATUS again. -NOT COMPLETED- Meet the test condition, and check the OBD STATUS again. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0714: ATF Temperature Sensor (Intermittent Failure)
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) in P, and wait for at least 6 hours.

Start the engine in P, and wait for at least 10 seconds. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0714

dtcs.description: ATF Temperature Sensor (Intermittent Failure) The input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor is attached to the outside of the transmission housing. The input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor generates a pulsing signal according to the speed of the idler gear on the input shaft (mainshaft). Using that signal, the powertrain control module (PCM) determines the speed of the input shaft (mainshaft). If no pulses occur with the input shaft (mainshaft) rotating, the PCM detects a malfunction that may be caused by an open, a temporary open, or a short to ground. Based on the velocity ratio measured by the output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor and the input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The vehicle speed measured by the input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor [INPUT SHAFT (MAINSHAFT) SPEED]/(divided by) the vehicle speed
measured by the output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor [OUTPUT SHAFT (COUNTERSHAFT) SPEED] is less than 0.156 for at least 10 seconds.

steps.instruction: Current ATF temperature check: Current ATF temperature check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Check the following parameters with the HDS: * ATF Temperature in the A/T Data List * ECT Sensor 2 in the PGM-FI Data List Are the ATF Temperature and ECT Sensor 2 equal to the ambient air temperature? -YES- Record the values of the ATF Temperature and ECT Sensor 2 with the HDS, then go to step 3. -NO- Record the value of the ATF Temperature with the HDS, then go to step 2.

ATF temperature difference check: ATF temperature difference check -1. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and wait for at least 30 minutes. -2. Compare the current ATF Temperature to the ATF Temperature recorded in step 1. Does the ATF Temperature change? -YES- Leave the engine off for at least 6 hours, then go to step 3. -NO- Replace the ATF temperature sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair.

ATF temperature sensor check: ATF temperature sensor check -1. Compare the values of the ATF Temperature Sensor and ECT Sensor 2. Is the value of the ATF Temperature Sensor higher than the value of ECT Sensor 2 by 58 °F (32 °C), or is the value of the ATF Temperature Sensor lower than the value of ECT Sensor 2 by 43 °F (24 °C)? -YES- Replace the ATF temperature sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Temperature Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair. -NO- The ATF temperature sensor is OK. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0716: Input Shaft (Mainshaft) Speed Sensor (Range/Performance)
Start the engine.

Drive the vehicle at a speed of 13 mph (20 km/h) or more for at least 10 seconds. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Control System DTCs P0716

dtcs.description: Input Shaft (Mainshaft) Speed Sensor (Range/Performance) The input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor is attached to the outside of the transmission housing. The input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor generates a pulsing signal according to the speed of the idler gear on the input shaft (mainshaft). Using that signal, the powertrain control module (PCM) determines the speed of the input shaft (mainshaft). If no pulses occur with the input shaft (mainshaft) rotating, the PCM detects a malfunction that may be caused by an open, a temporary open, or a short to ground. Based on the correlation between the vehicle speed measured by the output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor and the input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: When the vehicle speed measured by the output shaft (countershaft) speed sensor [OUTPUT SHAFT (COUNTERSHAFT) SPEED] is 13 mph (20 km/h)
or more, the vehicle speed measured by the input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor [INPUT SHAFT (MAINSHAFT) SPEED] is 1 mph (2 km/h) or less for
at least 10 seconds.

steps.instruction: DTC check: DTC check -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with the HDS. -3. Block the rear wheels and raise the front of the vehicle, or raise the vehicle on a lift. Make sure it is securely supported, and allow the front wheels to rotate freely. -4. Start the engine, disable the VSA by pressing the VSA OFF button, run the vehicle with the shift lever in D, and keep the vehicle speed at speeds over 12 mph (20 km/h) for at least 10 seconds. Slow down, and stop the wheels. -5. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0718 indicated? -YES- Do the troubleshooting for DTC P0718. -NO- Go to step 2.

Problem verification: Problem verification -1. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0716 indicated? -YES- The failure is duplicated. Go to step 3. -NO- Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor connection check: Input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor connection check Are the connection and installation OK? -YES- The connection and installation are OK. Replace the input shaft (mainshaft) speed sensor  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Sensors and Switches - Transmission and Drivetrain/Sensors and Switches - A/T/Transmission Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Input Shaft (Mainshaft) Speed Sensor Removal and Installation, then do the repair verification. -NO- Repair faulty connection or installation, then do the repair verification. Repair verification procedure: -1. Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). -2. Clear the DTC with HDS. -3. Start the engine, disable the VSA by pressing the VSA OFF button, run the vehicle with the shift lever in D, and keep the vehicle speed at speeds over 12 mph (20 km/h) for at least 10 seconds. Slow down, and stop the wheels. -4. Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0716 indicated? -YES- Go to step 1. -NO- Troubleshooting is complete. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0717: Input Shaft (Mainshaft) Speed Sensor (No Signal Input)
Start the engine.

Drive the vehicle at a speed of 13 mph (20 km/h) or more for at least 10 seconds. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow.
